netapp.ontap.na_ontap_active_directory.description: Configure Active Directory. REST requires ONTAP 9.12.1 or later.

state.description: Whether the Active Directory should exist or not

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

account_name.description: Active Directory account NetBIOS name. Modifying an existing account name is not supported. The account must be deleted and recreated.

admin_password.description: Administrator password required for Active Directory account creation.

admin_username.description: Administrator username required for Active Directory account creation.

force_account_overwrite.description: If true and a machine account with the same name as specified in 'account-name' exists in Active Directory, it will be overwritten and reused.

organizational_unit.description: Organizational unit under which the Active Directory account will be created. Modifying the organizational unit is not supported. The object must be deleted and recreated.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_active_directory_d.short_description: NetApp ONTAP configure active directory preferred domain controllers
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_active_directory_d.description: Configure Active Directory preferred Domain Controllers.

state.description: Whether the Active Directory preferred Domain Controllers configuration should exist or not

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

server_ip.description: IP address of the preferred DC. The address can be either an IPv4 or an IPv6 address.

skip_config_validation.description: Skip the validation of the specified preferred DC configuration.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_aggregate.description: Create, delete, or manage aggregates on ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified aggregate should exist or not.

service_state.description: Whether the specified aggregate should be enabled or disabled. Creates aggregate if doesnt exist. Supported from 9.11.1 or later in REST.

name.description: The name of the aggregate to manage.

from_name.description: Name of the aggregate to be renamed.

nodes.description: Node(s) for the aggregate to be created on.  If no node specified, mgmt lif home will be used. ZAPI only - if multiple nodes specified an aggr stripe will be made. With REST, only one node can be specified.  If disk_count is present, node name is required.

disk_type.description: Type of disk to use to build aggregate. Not supported with REST - see C(disk_class). SSD-NVM, SSD-CAP were added with ONTAP 9.6. VMLUN was added with ONTAP 9.9.

disk_class.description: Class of disk to use to build aggregate. C(capacity_flash) is listed in swagger, but rejected as invalid by ONTAP.

disk_count.description: Number of disks to place into the aggregate, including parity disks. The disks in this newly-created aggregate come from the spare disk pool. The smallest disks in this pool join the aggregate first, unless the C(disk-size) argument is provided. Either C(disk-count) or C(disks) must be supplied. Range [0..2^31-1]. Required when C(state=present). Modifiable only if specified disk_count is larger than current disk_count. Cannot create raidgroup with 1 disk when using raid type raid4. If the disk_count % raid_size == 1, only disk_count/raid_size * raid_size will be added. If disk_count is 6, raid_type is raid4, raid_size 4, all 6 disks will be added. If disk_count is 5, raid_type is raid4, raid_size 4, 5/4 * 4 = 4 will be added. 1 will not be added. With REST, C(nodes) is required if C(disk_count) is present.

disk_size.description: Disk size to use in 4K block size.  Disks within 10% of specified size will be used. With REST, this is converted to bytes using 4096.  Use C(disk_size_with_unit) to skip the conversion.

disk_size_with_unit.description: Disk size to use in the specified unit. It is a positive integer number followed by unit of T/G/M/K. For example, 72G, 1T and 32M. Or the unit can be omitted for bytes (REST also accepts B). This option is ignored if a specific list of disks is specified through the "disks" parameter. You must only use one of either "disk-size" or "disk-size-with-unit" parameters. With REST, this is converted to bytes, assuming K=1024.

raid_size.description: Sets the maximum number of drives per raid group.

raid_type.description: Specifies the type of RAID groups to use in the new aggregate. raid_0 is only available on ONTAP Select.

unmount_volumes.description: If set to "true", this option specifies that all of the volumes hosted by the given aggregate are to be unmounted before the offline operation is executed. By default, the system will reject any attempt to offline an aggregate that hosts one or more online volumes. Not supported with REST, by default REST unmount volumes when trying to offline aggregate.

disks.description: Specific list of disks to use for the new aggregate. To create a "mirrored" aggregate with a specific list of disks, both 'disks' and 'mirror_disks' options must be supplied. Additionally, the same number of disks must be supplied in both lists. Not supported with REST.

is_mirrored.description: Specifies that the new aggregate be mirrored (have two plexes). If set to true, then the indicated disks will be split across the two plexes. By default, the new aggregate will not be mirrored. This option cannot be used when a specific list of disks is supplied with either the 'disks' or 'mirror_disks' options.

mirror_disks.description: List of mirror disks to use. It must contain the same number of disks specified in 'disks'. Not supported with REST.

spare_pool.description: Specifies the spare pool from which to select spare disks to use in creation of a new aggregate. Not supported with REST.

wait_for_online.description: Set this parameter to 'true' for synchronous execution during create (wait until aggregate status is online). Set this parameter to 'false' for asynchronous execution. For asynchronous, execution exits as soon as the request is sent, without checking aggregate status. Ignored with REST (always wait).

time_out.description: time to wait for aggregate creation in seconds. default is set to 100 seconds.

object_store_name.description: Name of the object store configuration attached to the aggregate.

allow_flexgroups.description: This optional parameter allows attaching object store to an aggregate containing FlexGroup constituents. The default value is false. Mixing FabricPools and non-FabricPools within a FlexGroup is not recommended. All aggregates hosting constituents of a FlexGroup should be attached to the object store.

snaplock_type.description: Type of snaplock for the aggregate being created.

ignore_pool_checks.description: only valid when I(disks) option is used. disks in a plex should belong to the same spare pool, and mirror disks to another spare pool. when set to true, these checks are ignored. Ignored with REST as I(disks) is not supported.

encryption.description: whether to enable software encryption. this is equivalent to -encrypt-with-aggr-key when using the CLI. requires a VE license.

tags.description: Tags are an optional way to track the uses of a resource. Tag values must be formatted as key:value strings, example ["team:csi", "environment:test"]

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

state.description: Specifies whether the AutoSupport daemon is to be enabled or disabled. When this setting is absent, delivery of all AutoSupport messages is turned off.

node_name.description: The name of the filer that owns the AutoSupport Configuration. Supported only with ZAPI.

transport.description: The name of the transport protocol used to deliver AutoSupport messages.

noteto.description: Specifies up to five recipients of short AutoSupport e-mail messages. Supported only with ZAPI.

post_url.description: The URL used to deliver AutoSupport messages via HTTP POST. Supported only with ZAPI.

mail_hosts.description: List of mail server(s) used to deliver AutoSupport messages via SMTP. Both host names and IP addresses may be used as valid input.

support.description: Specifies whether AutoSupport notification to technical support is enabled.

from_address.description: specify the e-mail address from which the node sends AutoSupport messages.

partner_addresses.description: Specifies up to five partner vendor recipients of full AutoSupport e-mail messages.

to_addresses.description: Specifies up to five recipients of full AutoSupport e-mail messages.

proxy_url.description: specify an HTTP or HTTPS proxy if the 'transport' parameter is set to HTTP or HTTPS and your organization uses a proxy. If authentication is required, use the format "username:password@host:port".

hostname_in_subject.description: Specify whether the hostname of the node is included in the subject line of the AutoSupport message. Supported only with ZAPI.

nht_data_enabled.description: Specify whether the disk health data is collected as part of the AutoSupport data. Supported only with ZAPI.

perf_data_enabled.description: Specify whether the performance data is collected as part of the AutoSupport data. Supported only with ZAPI.

retry_count.description: Specify the maximum number of delivery attempts for an AutoSupport message. Supported only with ZAPI.

reminder_enabled.description: Specify whether AutoSupport reminders are enabled or disabled. Supported only with ZAPI.

max_http_size.description: Specify delivery size limit for the HTTP transport protocol (in bytes). Supported only with ZAPI.

max_smtp_size.description: Specify delivery size limit for the SMTP transport protocol (in bytes). Supported only with ZAPI.

private_data_removed.description: Specify the removal of customer-supplied data. Supported only with ZAPI.

local_collection_enabled.description: Specify whether collection of AutoSupport data when the AutoSupport daemon is disabled. Supported only with ZAPI.

ondemand_enabled.description: Specify whether the AutoSupport OnDemand Download feature is enabled.

validate_digital_certificate.description: When set to true each node will validate the digital certificates that it receives. Supported only with ZAPI.

is_minimal.description: Specifies whether the system information is collected in compliant form, to remove private data or in complete form, to enhance diagnostics. Supported only with REST.

smtp_encryption.description: The encryption protocol used to deliver AutoSupport messages via SMTP to the configured mail_hosts. Supported only with REST.

force.description: Set the force flag to true to modify some of the AutoSupport configurations that are otherwise blocked when the automatic update feature is enabled. Without this flag set to true, an attempt to disable AutoSupport, modify the transport to SMTP, or disable the AutoSupport OnDemand feature fails if the automatic update feature is enabled.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_autosupport_invoke.description: Send an AutoSupport message from a node

name.description: The name of the node to send the message to. Not specifying this option invokes AutoSupport on all nodes in the cluster.

autosupport_message.description: Text sent in the subject line of the AutoSupport message. message is deprecated and will be removed to avoid a conflict with an Ansible internal variable.

type.description: Type of AutoSupport Collection to Issue.

uri.description: send the AutoSupport message to the destination you specify instead of the configured destination.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_autoupdate_config.short_description: NetApp ONTAP module to manage configurations for automatic updates.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_autoupdate_config.description: Modify the configuration for a specified automatic update.

state.description: Update the configuration, only present is supported.

category.description: Specifies the category for the configuration row. Examples - firmware, sp_fw, system, security.

action.description: Specifies the action to be taken by the alert source as specified by the user.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_autoupdate_support.short_description: NetApp ONTAP enable auto update status.

force.description: Set the force flag to true to enable the automatic update feature regardless of how AutoSupport is configured. Without this flag set to true, an attempt to enable the automatic update feature fails unless AutoSupport is enabled, its transport is HTTPS, and the AutoSupport OnDemand feature is enabled.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_bgp_config.description: Create/ modify/ delete BGP configuration for a node.

state.description: Specifies whether to create/ update or delete the border gateway protocol (BGP) configuration for a node.

node.description: Specifies the node on which configuration details will be managed.

asn.description: Specifies the autonomous system number (ASN). The ASN attribute is a positive integer of the range from 1 to 4,294,967,295. It should typically be chosen from RFC6996 "Autonomous System (AS) Reservation for Private Use" or the AS number assigned to the operator's organization.

hold_time.description: Specifies the hold time in seconds. The default value is 180.

router_id.description: Specifies the local router ID. The router-id value takes the form of an IPv4 address. The default router-id will be initialized using a local IPv4 address in admin vserver if not given for create operation.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_bgp_peer_group.short_description: NetApp ONTAP module to create, modify or delete bgp peer group.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_bgp_peer_group.description: Create, modify or delete bgp peer group.

state.description: Create or delete BGP peer group.

name.description: Name of the BGP peer group.

from_name.description: Name of the existing BGP peer group to be renamed to C(name).

ipspace.description: IPSpace name, cannot be modified after creation.

local.description: Information describing the local interface that is being used to peer with a router using BGP. When creating BGP peer group, an existing BGP interface is used by specifying the interface, or create a new one by specifying the port and IP address. Cannot be modified after creation.

peer.description: Information describing the router to peer with

use_peer_as_next_hop.description: Specifies whether the peer group uses the peer address as a next hop route. This field requires ONTAP version 9.9 or later.

state.description: Whether the specified broadcast domain should exist or not.

name.description: Specify the broadcast domain name.

from_name.description: Specify the broadcast domain name to be split into new broadcast domain.

mtu.description: Specify the required mtu for the broadcast domain.

ipspace.description: Specify the required ipspace for the broadcast domain. With ZAPI, a domain ipspace cannot be modified after the domain has been created. With REST, a domain ipspace can be modified. This option is required while using REST.

from_ipspace.description: if used with C(from_name), it will try to find broadcast domain C(from_name) in C(from_ipspace), split action either rename broadcast_domain and ipspace or create a new broadcast domain. If not C(from_name) present, it will try to find C(name) broadcast domain in C(from_ipspace) and modify ipspace using C(ipspace). Only supported with REST.

ports.description: Specify the ports associated with this broadcast domain. Should be comma separated. It represents the expected state of a list of ports at any time. Add a port if it is specified in expected state but not in current state. Delete a port if it is specified in current state but not in expected state. For split action, it represents the ports to be split from current broadcast domain and added to the new broadcast domain. If all ports are removed or split from a broadcast domain, the broadcast domain will be deleted automatically. With REST, if exact match of ports found with C(from_name), split action will rename the broadcast domain using C(name). With REST, if partial match of ports with C(from_name), split action will create a new broadcast domain using C(name) and move partial matched ports from C(from_name) to C(name). With REST, if C(ports) not in C(from_name), split action will create a new broadcast domain using C(name) with C(ports).

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_broadcast_domain_p.short_description: NetApp ONTAP manage broadcast domain ports
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_broadcast_domain_p.description: Add or remove ONTAP broadcast domain ports.  Existing ports that are not listed are kept.

state.description: Whether the specified broadcast domain should exist or not.

ipspace.description: Specify the ipspace for the broadcast domain

ports.description: Specify the list of ports to add to or remove from this broadcast domain.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cg.short_description: NetApp ONTAP module to manage operations related to consistency groups.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cg.description: Create a consistency group with one or more consistency groups having new or existing volumes. Modify or delete an existing consistency group.

state.description: Specifies whether to create, modify, or delete a consistency group.

name.description: Specifies the name of the consistency group. The consistency group name must be unique within an SVM.

vserver.description: Specifies the SVM in which the consistency group is located.

volumes.description: List of volumes to be included in the consistency group. The volumes array can be used to create new volumes in the consistency group, add existing volumes to the consistency group. A volume can only be associated with one direct parent consistency group. Removing all volumes from a consistency group will automatically delete the consistency group.

luns.description: The LUNs array can be used to create or modify LUNs in a consistency group on a new or existing volume that is a member of the consistency group.

namespaces.description: The namespaces array can be used to create or modify namespaces in a consistency group.

consistency_groups.description: List of consistency groups to be included in the consistency group.

qos_policy.description: Specifies the QoS policy for the consistency group. Only supported when provisioning new objects.

snapshot_policy.description: Specifies the snapshot policy for the consistency group.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cg_snapshot.short_description: NetApp ONTAP manage consistency group snapshot
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cg_snapshot.description: Create or delete consistency group snapshot for ONTAP volumes.

state.description: Specifies whether to create  or delete the snapshot. Choice 'absent' is valid only with REST.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver.

volumes.description: A list of volumes in this filer that is part of this CG operation. Required with ZAPI.

consistency_group.description: Name of the consistency group for which snapshot needs to be created or deleted. Valid only with REST.

snapshot.description: The provided name of the snapshot that is created in each volume.

timeout.description: Timeout selector. Not supported with REST.

snapmirror_label.description: A human readable SnapMirror label to be attached with the consistency group snapshot copies.

comment.description: Comment for the snapshot copy. Only supported with REST.

consistency_type.description: Type of consistency guarantee for the snapshot. Only supported with REST. If not specified, ONTAP uses its default consistency type.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs.description: Create or destroy or modify(path) cifs-share on ONTAP.

path.description: The file system path that is shared through this CIFS share. The path is the full, user visible path relative to the vserver root, and it might be crossing junction mount points. The path is in UTF8 and uses forward slash as directory separator.

vserver.description: Vserver containing the CIFS share.

name.description: The name of the CIFS share. The CIFS share name is a UTF-8 string with the following characters being illegal; control characters from 0x00 to 0x1F, both inclusive, 0x22 (double quotes)

share_properties.description: The list of properties for the CIFS share. Not supported with REST. share-properties are separate fields in the REST API. You can achieve this functionality by setting C(access_based_enumeration), C(change_notify), C(encryption), C(home_directory), C(oplocks), C(show_snapshot), C(continuously_available) and C(namespace_caching).

symlink_properties.description: The list of symlink properties for this CIFS share. Not supported with REST, this option is replaced with C(unix_symlink) in REST.

state.description: Whether the specified CIFS share should exist or not.

vscan_fileop_profile.description: Profile_set of file_ops to which vscan on access scanning is applicable. REST support requires ONTAP 9.15.1 or later.

unix_symlink.description: The list of unix_symlink properties for this CIFS share This option only supported with REST.

access_based_enumeration.description: If enabled, all folders inside this share are visible to a user based on that individual user access right; prevents the display of folders or other shared resources that the user does not have access to. This option only supported with REST.

allow_unencrypted_access.description: Specifies whether or not the SMB2 clients are allowed to access the encrypted share. This option requires REST and ONTAP 9.11.0 or later.

change_notify.description: Specifies whether CIFS clients can request for change notifications for directories on this share. This option only supported with REST.

encryption.description: Specifies that SMB encryption must be used when accessing this share. Clients that do not support encryption are not able to access this share. This option only supported with REST.

home_directory.description: Specifies whether or not the share is a home directory share, where the share and path names are dynamic. ONTAP home directory functionality automatically offer each user a dynamic share to their home directory without creating an individual SMB share for each user. This feature enable us to configure a share that maps to different directories based on the user that connects to it Instead of creating a separate shares for each user, a single share with a home directory parameters can be created. In a home directory share, ONTAP dynamically generates the share-name and share-path by substituting %w, %u, and %d variables with the corresponding Windows user name, UNIX user name, and domain name, respectively. This option only supported with REST and cannot modify.

namespace_caching.description: Specifies whether or not the SMB clients connecting to this share can cache the directory enumeration results returned by the CIFS servers. This option requires REST and ONTAP 9.10.1 or later.

oplocks.description: Specify whether opportunistic locks are enabled on this share. "Oplocks" allow clients to lock files and cache content locally, which can increase performance for file operations. Only supported with REST.

show_snapshot.description: Specifies whether or not the Snapshot copies can be viewed and traversed by clients. This option requires REST and ONTAP 9.10.1 or later.

continuously_available.description: Specifies whether or not the clients connecting to this share can open files in a persistent manner. Files opened in this way are protected from disruptive events, such as, failover and giveback. This option requires REST and ONTAP 9.10.1 or later.

browsable.description: Specifies whether or not the Windows clients can browse the share. This option requires REST and ONTAP 9.13.1 or later.

show_previous_versions.description: Specifies that the previous version can be viewed and restored from the client. This option requires REST and ONTAP 9.13.1 or later.

offline_files.description: Allows Windows clients to cache data on this share. This option is only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.10 or later.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_acl.description: Create or destroy or modify cifs-share-access-controls on ONTAP

permission.description: The access rights that the user or group has on the defined CIFS share.

share_name.description: The name of the cifs-share-access-control to manage.

state.description: Whether the specified CIFS share acl should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

user_or_group.description: The user or group name for which the permissions are listed.

type.description: The type (also known as user-group-type) of the user or group to add to the ACL. Type is required for create, delete and modify unix-user or unix-group to/from the ACL in ZAPI.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_local_group.short_description: NetApp Ontap - create, delete or modify CIFS local group.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_local_group.description: Create, delete or modify CIFS local group.

state.description: Whether the specified member should be part of the CIFS local group

vserver.description: Specifies the vserver that owns the CIFS local group

name.description: Specifies name of the CIFS local group

from_name.description: Specifies the existing cifs local group name. This option is used to rename cifs local group.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_local_group_m.short_description: NetApp Ontap - Add or remove CIFS local group member
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_local_group_m.description: Add or remove CIFS local group member

state.description: Whether the specified member should be part of the CIFS local group

vserver.description: Specifies the vserver that owns the CIFS local group

group.description: Specifies name of the CIFS local group

member.description: Specifies the name of the member

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

state.description: Whether the specified CIFS share should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the local cifs user

vserver.description: the name of the data vserver to use.

account_disabled.description: Whether the local cifs user is disabled or not

description.description: the description for the local cifs user

full_name.description: the full name for the local cifs user

set_password.description: Modify the existing user password Module is not idempotent when set to True

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_local_user_mo.short_description: NetApp ONTAP modify local CIFS user.

name.description: The name of the local cifs user

vserver.description: the name of the data vserver to use.

is_account_disabled.description: Whether the local cifs user is disabled or not

description.description: the description for the local cifs user

full_name.description: the full name for the local cifs user

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_local_user_se.short_description: NetApp ONTAP set local CIFS user password
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_local_user_se.description: Sets the password for the specified local user. NOTE - This module is not idempotent. Password must meet the following criteria The password must be at least six characters in length. The password must not contain user account name. The password must contain characters from three of the following four English uppercase characters (A through Z) English lowercase characters (a through z) Base 10 digits (0 through 9) Special characters

vserver.description: name of the vserver.

user_name.description: The name of the local CIFS user to set the password for.

user_password.description: The password to set for the local CIFS user.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_privileges.description: Add/modify/reset privileges of the local or Active Directory user or group.

state.description: Specifies whether to add/update or reset the specified CIFS privileges.

name.description: The name of the local or Active Directory user or group name.

vserver.description: the name of the data vserver to use.

privileges.description: Specifies the list of privileges to be retained for a user or group. SeTcbPrivilege - Allows user to act as part of the operating system SeBackupPrivilege - Allows user to back up files and directories, overriding any ACLs SeRestorePrivilege - Allows user to restore files and directories, overriding any ACLs SeTakeOwnershipPrivilege - Allows user to take ownership of files or other objects SeSecurityPrivilege - Allows user to manage auditing and viewing/dumping/clearing the security log SeChangeNotifyPrivilege - Allows user to bypass traverse checking

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_server.description: Creating / deleting and modifying the CIFS server .

state.description: Whether the specified cifs_server should exist or not.

name.description: Specifies the cifs_server name.

comment.description: A descriptive text comment for the CIFS server. SMB clients can see the CIFS server comment when browsing servers on the network. Only supported with REST.

admin_user_name.description: Specifies the cifs server admin username. When used with absent, the account will be deleted if admin_password is also provided.

admin_password.description: Specifies the cifs server admin password. When used with absent, the account will be deleted if admin_user_name is also provided.

domain.description: The Fully Qualified Domain Name of the Windows Active Directory this CIFS server belongs to.

workgroup.description: The NetBIOS name of the domain or workgroup this CIFS server belongs to.

ou.description: The Organizational Unit (OU) within the Windows Active Directory this CIFS server belongs to.

default_site.description: Specifies the site within the Active Directory domain to associate with the CIFS server if Data ONTAP cannot determine an appropriate site. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.13.1 or later.

force.description: When state is present, if this is set and a machine account with the same name as specified in 'name' exists in the Active Directory, it will be overwritten and reused. When state is absent, if this is set, the local CIFS configuration is deleted regardless of communication errors. For REST, it requires ontap version 9.11.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

from_name.description: Specifies the existing cifs_server name. This option is used to rename cifs_server. Supported only in REST and requires force to be set to True. Requires ontap version 9.11.0. if the service is running, it will be stopped to perform the rename action, and automatically restarts. if the service is stopped, it will be briefly restarted after the rename action, and stopped again.

encrypt_dc_connection.description: Specifies whether encryption is required for domain controller connections. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.8 or later. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

kdc_encryption.description: Specifies whether AES-128 and AES-256 encryption is enabled for all Kerberos-based communication with the Active Directory KDC. Only supported with REST. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

smb_encryption.description: Determine whether SMB encryption is required for incoming CIFS traffic. Only supported with REST. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

smb_signing.description: Specifies whether signing is required for incoming CIFS traffic. Only supported with REST. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

restrict_anonymous.description: Specifies what level of access an anonymous user is granted. Only supported with REST.

aes_netlogon_enabled.description: Specifies whether or not an AES session key is enabled for the Netlogon channel. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.10.1 or later.

ldap_referral_enabled.description: Specifies whether or not LDAP referral chasing is enabled for AD LDAP connections. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.10.1 or later. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

use_ldaps.description: Specifies whether or not to use use LDAPS for secure Active Directory LDAP connections. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.10.1 or later. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

use_start_tls.description: Specifies whether or not to use SSL/TLS for allowing secure LDAP communication with Active Directory LDAP servers. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.10.1 or later. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

try_ldap_channel_binding.description: Specifies whether or not channel binding is attempted in the case of TLS/LDAPS. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.10.1 or later. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

session_security.description: Specifies client session security for AD LDAP connections. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.10.1 or later. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

lm_compatibility_level.description: Specifies CIFS server minimum security level, also known as the LMCompatibilityLevel. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.8 or later. Use na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security with ZAPI.

is_multichannel_enabled.description: Specifies whether the CIFS server supports Multichannel or not. Only supported with REST and requires ontap version 9.10 or later.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_unix_symlink_.short_description: NetApp ONTAP module to manage UNIX symbolic link mapping for CIFS clients.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cifs_unix_symlink_.description: Create/ modify/ delete a UNIX symbolic link mapping for a CIFS client.

state.description: Whether the specified symlink mapping should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

unix_path.description: Specifies the UNIX path prefix to be matched for the mapping. It must begin and end with a forward slash (/).

share_name.description: Specifies the CIFS share name on the destination CIFS server to which the UNIX symbolic link is pointing.

cifs_server.description: Specifies the destination CIFS server (DNS name, IP address, or NetBIOS name). This field is mandatory if the locality of the symbolic link is 'widelink'.

cifs_path.description: Specifies the CIFS path on the destination to which the symbolic link maps. Note that this value is specified by using a UNIX-style path. It must begin and end with a forward slash (/).

locality.description: Specifies whether the CIFS symbolic link is a local link or wide link. The default setting is local. The following values are supported local - Local symbolic link maps only to the same CIFS share. widelink - Wide symbolic link maps to any CIFS share on the network.

home_directory.description: Specify if the destination share is a home directory. The default value is false.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cli_timeout.short_description: NetApp ONTAP module to set the CLI inactivity timeout value.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cli_timeout.description: Modify the timeout value for CLI sessions.

state.description: Modify timeout value, only present is supported.

timeout.description: Specifies the timeout value, in minutes. To prevent CLI sessions from timing out, specify a value of 0 (zero).

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cluster.short_description: NetApp ONTAP cluster - create a cluster and add/remove nodes.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cluster.description: Create ONTAP cluster. Add or remove cluster nodes using cluster_ip_address. Adding a node requires ONTAP 9.3 or better. Removing a node requires ONTAP 9.4 or better.

state.description: Whether the specified cluster should exist (deleting a cluster is not supported). Whether the node identified by its cluster_ip_address should be in the cluster or not.

cluster_name.description: The name of the cluster to manage.

cluster_ip_address.description: intra cluster IP address of the node to be added or removed.

single_node_cluster.description: Whether the cluster is a single node cluster.  Ignored for 9.3 or older versions. If present, it was observed that 'Cluster' interfaces were deleted, whatever the value with ZAPI.

cluster_location.description: Cluster location, only relevant if performing a modify action.

cluster_contact.description: Cluster contact, only relevant if performing a modify action.

node_name.description: Name of the node to be added or removed from the cluster. Be aware that when adding a node, '-' are converted to '_' by the ONTAP backend. When creating a cluster, C(node_name) is ignored. When adding a node using C(cluster_ip_address), C(node_name) is optional. When used to remove a node, C(cluster_ip_address) and C(node_name) are mutually exclusive.

time_out.description: time to wait for cluster creation in seconds. Error out if task is not completed in defined time. if 0, the request is asynchronous. default is set to 3 minutes.

force.description: forcibly remove a node that is down and cannot be brought online to remove its shared resources.

timezone.description: timezone for the cluster. Only supported by REST.

certificate.description: Certificate used by cluster and node management interfaces for TLS connection requests. Only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.10 or later.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cluster_ha.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Manage HA status for cluster
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cluster_ha.description: Enable or disable HA on a cluster

state.description: Whether HA on cluster should be enabled or disabled.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_cluster_peer.description: Create/Delete cluster peer relations on ONTAP Modify remote intercluster addresses in cluster peer relation on ONTAP

state.description: Whether the specified cluster peer should exist or not.

source_intercluster_lifs.description: List of intercluster addresses of the source cluster. Used as peer-addresses in destination cluster. All these intercluster lifs should belong to the source cluster.

dest_intercluster_lifs.description: List of intercluster addresses of the destination cluster. Used as peer-addresses in source cluster. All these intercluster lifs should belong to the destination cluster.

passphrase.description: The arbitrary passphrase that matches the one given to the peer cluster.

source_cluster_name.description: The name of the source cluster name in the peer relation to be modified or deleted. Required for deleting peer relation and for modifying source_intercluster_lifs.

dest_cluster_name.description: The name of the destination cluster name in the peer relation to be modified or deleted. Required for deleting peer relation and for modifying dest_intercluster_lifs.

dest_hostname.description: DEPRECATED - please use C(peer_options). Destination cluster IP or hostname which needs to be peered. Required to complete the peering process at destination cluster.

dest_username.description: DEPRECATED - please use C(peer_options). Destination username. Optional if this is same as source username or if a certificate is used.

dest_password.description: DEPRECATED - please use C(peer_options). Destination password. Optional if this is same as source password or if a certificate is used..

ipspace.description: IPspace of the local intercluster LIFs. Assumes Default IPspace if not provided.

encryption_protocol_proposed.description: Encryption protocol to be used for inter-cluster communication. Only available on ONTAP 9.5 or later.

local_name_for_peer.description: Specifies local name of the peer Cluster in the relationship. By default the system will generate the same name as cluster name.

local_name_for_source.description: Specifies local name of the source Cluster in the relationship. By default the system will generate the same name as cluster name.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_command.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Run any cli command, the username provided needs to have console login permission.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_command.description: Run system-cli commands on ONTAP. Can't be used with cert authentication and domain authentication accounts. Requires ontapi and console permissions. Console is not supported for data vservers. Requires write permissions, even for show commands!  ONTAP reports "Insufficient privileges" and "user 'xxxxx' does not have write access to this resource" for a readonly user.

command.description: a comma separated list containing the command and arguments.

privilege.description: privilege level at which to run the command.

return_dict.description: Returns a parsesable dictionary instead of raw XML output C(result_value) C(status) > passed, failed. C(stdout) > command output in plaintext. C(stdout_lines) > list of command output lines. C(stdout_lines_filter) > empty list or list of command output lines matching I(include_lines) or I(exclude_lines) parameters. C(xml_dict) > JSON representation of what the CLI returned.

vserver.description: If running as vserver admin, you must give a I(vserver) or module will fail

include_lines.description: applied only when I(return_dict) is true return only lines containing string pattern in C(stdout_lines_filter)

exclude_lines.description: applied only when I(return_dict) is true return only lines containing string pattern in C(stdout_lines_filter)

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_debug.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Debug netapp-lib import and connection.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_debug.description: Display issues related to importing netapp-lib and connection with diagnose

vserver.description: The vserver name to test for ZAPI tunneling.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_disk_options.short_description: NetApp ONTAP modify storage disk options
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_disk_options.description: Modify a nodes storage disk options Requires ONTAP 9.6 or greater

node.description: The node to modify a disk option for

bkg_firmware_update.description: Whether or not background disk firmware updates should be enabled

autocopy.description: Whether or not disk auto copies should be enabled

autoassign.description: Whether or not disks should be automatically assigned to a node

autoassign_policy.description: the auto assign policy to use

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_disks.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Assign disks to nodes
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_disks.description: Assign disks to a node. Disk autoassign must be turned off before using this module to prevent the disks being reassigned automatically by the cluster. This can be done through na_ontap_disk_options or via the cli "disk option modify -node <node_name> -autoassign off". If min_spares is not specified min_spares default is 1 if SSD or 2 for any other disk type. If disk_count is not specified all unassigned disks will be assigned to the node specified.

node.description: The node that we want to assign/unassign disks.

disk_count.description: Total number of disks a node should own.

disk_type.description: Assign specified type of disk (or set of disks).

min_spares.description: Minimum spares required per type for the node.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_dns.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Create, delete, modify DNS servers.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_dns.description: Create, delete, modify DNS servers. With REST, the module is currently limited to data vservers for delete or modify operations.

state.description: Whether the DNS servers should be enabled for the given vserver.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use. With REST, for cluster scoped DNS, omit this option or set it to NULL for ONTAP 9.13.1 or later and provide cluster vserver as its value for ONTAP 9.12.1 or earlier. With ZAPI or REST, for cluster scoped DNS, this can also be set to the cluster vserver name.

domains.description: List of DNS domains such as 'sales.bar.com'. The first domain is the one that the Vserver belongs to.

nameservers.description: List of IPv4 addresses of name servers such as '123.123.123.123'.

skip_validation.description: By default, all nameservers are checked to validate they are available to resolve. If you DNS servers are not yet installed or momentarily not available, you can set this option to 'true' to bypass the check for all servers specified in nameservers field. With REST, requires ONTAP 9.9.1 or later and ignored for cluster DNS operations.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_domain_tunnel.description: Create, delete or modify the domain tunnel.

state.description: Whether the domain tunnel should exist or not.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver that the domain tunnel should be created or deleted on.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_efficiency_policy.short_description: NetApp ONTAP manage efficiency policies (sis policies)

state.description: Whether the specified efficiency policy should exist or not.

policy_name.description: the name of the efficiency policy

comment.description: A brief description of the policy.

duration.description: The duration in hours for which the scheduled efficiency operation should run. After this time expires, the efficiency operation will be stopped even if the operation is incomplete. If '-' is specified as the duration, the efficiency operation will run till it completes. Otherwise, the duration has to be an integer greater than 0. By default, the operation runs till it completes.

enabled.description: If the value is true, the efficiency policy is active in this cluster. If the value is false this policy will not be activated by the schedulers and hence will be inactive.

policy_type.description: The policy type reflects the reason a volume using this policy will start processing a changelog. (Changelog processing is identifying and eliminating duplicate blocks which were written since the changelog was last processed.) threshold Changelog processing occurs once the changelog reaches a certain percent full. scheduled Changelog processing will be triggered by time.

qos_policy.description: QoS policy for the efficiency operation. background efficiency operation will run in background with minimal or no impact on data serving client operations, best-effort efficiency operations may have some impact on data serving client operations.

schedule.description: Cron type job schedule name. When the associated policy is set on a volume, the efficiency operation will be triggered for the volume on this schedule. These schedules can be created using the na_ontap_job_schedule module

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

changelog_threshold_percent.description: Specifies the percentage at which the changelog will be processed for a threshold type of policy, tested once each hour.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ems_config.short_description: NetApp ONTAP module to modify EMS configuration.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ems_config.description: Configure event notification and logging for the cluster.

state.description: modify EMS configuration, only present is supported.

mail_from.description: The email address that the event notification system uses as the "From" address for email notifications.

mail_server.description: The name or IP address of the SMTP server that the event notification system uses to send email notification of events.

proxy_url.description: HTTP or HTTPS proxy server URL used by rest-api type EMS notification destinations if your organization uses a proxy.

proxy_user.description: User name for the HTTP or HTTPS proxy server if authentication is required.

proxy_password.description: Password for HTTP or HTTPS proxy.

pubsub_enabled.description: Indicates whether or not events are published to the Publish/Subscribe messaging broker. Requires ONTAP 9.10 or later.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ems_destination.short_description: NetApp ONTAP configuration for EMS event destination

state.description: Whether the destination should be present or not.

name.description: Name of the EMS destination.

destination.description: Destination - content depends on the type.

filters.description: List of filters that destination is linked to.

syslog.description: The parameter is specified when the EMS destination type is C(syslog).

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ems_filter.description: Create, delete, or modify EMS filters on NetApp ONTAP. This module only supports REST.

state.description: Whether the specified user should exist or not.

name.description: Name of the EMS Filter

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_export_policy.description: Create or destroy or rename export-policies on ONTAP

state.description: Whether the specified export policy should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the export-policy to manage.

from_name.description: The name of the export-policy to be renamed.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_export_policy_rule.short_description: NetApp ONTAP manage export policy rules
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_export_policy_rule.description: Create or delete or modify export rules in ONTAP

state.description: Whether the specified export policy rule should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the export policy this rule will be added to (or modified, or removed from).

client_match.description: List of Client Match host names, IP Addresses, Netgroups, or Domains.

anonymous_user_id.description: User name or ID to which anonymous users are mapped. Default value is '65534'.

ro_rule.description: List of Read only access specifications for the rule

rw_rule.description: List of Read Write access specifications for the rule

super_user_security.description: List of Read Write access specifications for the rule

allow_suid.description: If 'true', NFS server will honor SetUID bits in SETATTR operation. Default value on creation is 'true'

protocol.description: List of Client access protocols. Default value is set to 'any' during create.

rule_index.description: Index of the export policy rule. When rule_index is not set, we try to find a rule with an exact match. If found, no action is taken with state set to present, and the rule is deleted with state set to absent. An error is reported if more than one rule is found. When rule_index is set and state is present, if a rule cannot be found with this index, we try to find a rule with an exact match and assign the index to this rule if found. If no match is found, a new rule is created. All attributes that are set are used for an exact match.  As a minimum, client_match, ro_rule, and rw_rule are required.

from_rule_index.description: index of the export policy rule to be re-indexed

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

ntfs_unix_security.description: NTFS export UNIX security options. With REST, supported from ONTAP 9.9.1 version.

force_delete_on_first_match.description: when rule_index is not set, the default is to report an error on multiple matches. when this option is set, one of the rules with an exact match is deleted when state is absent. ignored when state is present.

chown_mode.description: Specifies who is authorized to change the ownership mode of a file. With REST, supported from ONTAP 9.9.1 version.

allow_device_creation.description: Specifies whether or not device creation is allowed. default is true. With REST, supported from ONTAP 9.9.1 version.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fcp.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Start, Stop and Enable FCP services.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fcp.description: Start, Stop and Enable FCP services.

state.description: Whether the FCP should be enabled or not.

status.description: Whether the FCP should be up or down

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fdsd.short_description: NetApp ONTAP create or remove a File Directory security descriptor.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fdsd.description: Create or remove a security descriptor.

state.description: Whether the specified security descriptor should exist or not.

name.description: Specifies the name of the security descriptor.

vserver.description: Specifies the vserver.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fdsp.short_description: NetApp ONTAP create or delete a file directory security policy
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fdsp.description: Create or delete a file directory security policy.

state.description: Whether the specified policy should exist or not.

name.description: Specifies the name of the policy.

vserver.description: Specifies the vserver for the security policy.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fdspt.short_description: NetApp ONTAP create, delete or modify File Directory security policy tasks
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fdspt.description: Create, modify or remove file directory security policy tasks.

state.description: Whether the specified Policy Task should exist or not.

name.description: Specifies the name of the policy the task will be associated with.

vserver.description: Specifies the vserver for the File Directory security policy.

access_control.description: Specifies access control of the task.

ntfs_sd.description: Specifies the NTFS security descriptor name.

path.description: Specifies the file or folder path of the task. In case of SLAG this path specify the volume or qtree mounted path.

security_type.description: Specifies the type of security. If not specified ONTAP will default to ntfs.

index_num.description: Specifies the index number of a task. Tasks are applied in order. A task with a larger index value is applied after a task with a lower \ index number. If you do not specify this optional parameter, new tasks are applied to the end of the index list.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fdss.short_description: NetApp ONTAP File Directory Security Set.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fdss.description: Set file directory security information. This module is not idempotent. If re-running this module to apply the currently assigned policy, the policy will be reassigned.

state.description: Whether the specified Policy Task should exist or not.

name.description: Specifies the security policy to apply.

vserver.description: Specifies the Vserver that contains the path to which the security policy is applied.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_file_directory_pol.short_description: NetApp ONTAP create, delete, or modify vserver security file-directory policy
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_file_directory_pol.description: Create, modify, or destroy vserver security file-directory policy Add or remove task from policy. Each time a policy/task is created/modified, automatically apply policy to vserver.

state.description: Whether the specified policy or task should exist or not.

vserver.description: Specifies the vserver for the policy.

policy_name.description: Specifies the name of the policy.

access_control.description: Specifies the access control of task to be applied.

path.description: Specifies the file or folder path of the task. If path is specified and the policy which the task is adding to, does not exist, it will create the policy first then add the task to it. If path is specified, delete operation only removes task from policy.

ignore_broken_symlinks.description: Skip Broken Symlinks. Options used when applying the policy to vserver.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_file_security_perm.short_description: NetApp ONTAP NTFS file security permissions
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_file_security_perm.description: Create, delete, or modify NTFS file security and audit policies of file or directory on NetApp ONTAP. Note that ACLs are mached based on ('user', 'access', 'access_control', 'apply_to'). In order to modify any of these 4 properties, the module deletes the ACL and creates a new one.

state.description: Whether the specified file security permission should exist or not. When absent, all ACLs are deleted, irrespective of the contents of C(acls). See C(access_control) to only delete all SLAG ACLS, or only delete file-directory ACLs. Inherited ACLs are ignored, they can't be deleted or modified.

path.description: The path of the file or directory on which to apply security permissions.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

owner.description: Specifies the owner of the NTFS security descriptor (SD). You can specify the owner using either a user name or security identifier (SID). The owner of the SD can modify the permissions on the file (or folder) or files (or folders) to which the SD is applied and can give other users the right to take ownership of the object or objects to which the SD is applied.

control_flags.description: Specifies the control flags in the SD. It is a Hexadecimal Value.

group.description: Specifies the owner's primary group. Specify the owner group using either a group name or SID.

ignore_paths.description: For each file or directory in the list, specifies that permissions on this file or directory cannot be replaced.

propagation_mode.description: Specifies how to propagate security settings to child subfolders and files. Defaults to propagate.

access_control.description: An Access Control Level specifies the access control of the task to be applied. Valid values are "file-directory" or "Storage-Level Access Guard (SLAG)". SLAG is used to apply the specified security descriptors with the task for the volume or qtree. Otherwise, the security descriptors are applied on files and directories at the specified path. The value slag is not supported on FlexGroups volumes. The default value is "file-directory". This field requires ONTAP 9.10.1 or later.  This defaults to "file_directory". When state is present, all ACLs not listed in C(acls) are deleted when this option is absent. If this option is present, only ACLs matching its value are deleted. When state is absent, all ACLs are deleted when this option is absent. If this option is present, only ACLs matching its value are deleted.

acls.description: A discretionary access security list (DACL) identifies the trustees that are allowed or denied access to a securable object. When a process tries to access a securable object, the system checks the access control entries (ACEs) in the object's DACL to determine whether to grant access to it.

validate_changes.description: ACLs may not be applied as expected. For instance, if Everyone is inherited will all permissions, additional users will be granted all permissions, regardless of the request. For this specific example, you can either delete the top level Everyone, or create a new ACL for Everyone at a lower level. When using C(rights), ONTAP translates them into C(advanced_rights) so the validation will always fail. Valid values are C(ignore), no checking; C(warn) to issue a warning; C(error) to fail the module. With SLAGS, ONTAP may split one ACL into two ACLs depending on the C(apply_to) settings.  To maintain idempotency, please provide 2 ACLs as input.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_file_security_perm.short_description: NetApp ONTAP file security permissions ACL
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_file_security_perm.description: Add, delete, or modify a file_security_permissions ACL on NetApp ONTAP. Note that ACLs are mached based on ('user', 'access', 'access_control', 'apply_to'). To modify any of these 4 properties, you would need to delete the ACL and create a new one. Or use C(netapp.ontap.na_ontap_file_security_permissions).

state.description: Whether the specified file security permissions ACL should exist or not.

path.description: The path of the file or directory on which to apply security permissions.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

access_control.description: An Access Control Level specifies the access control of the task to be applied. Valid values are "file-directory" or "Storage-Level Access Guard (SLAG)". SLAG is used to apply the specified security descriptors with the task for the volume or qtree. Otherwise, the security descriptors are applied on files and directories at the specified path. The value slag is not supported on FlexGroups volumes. The default value is "file-directory". This field requires ONTAP 9.10.1 or later.  This defaults to "file_directory".

access.description: An ACE is an element in an access control list (ACL). An ACL can have zero or more ACEs. Each ACE controls or monitors access to an object by a specified trustee.

acl_user.description: Specifies the account to which the ACE applies. Specify either name or SID. As of 22.0.0, the module is not idempotent when using a SID. Note - we cannot use C(user) as if conflicts with the option for the admin user.

rights.description: Specifies the access right controlled by the ACE for the account specified. The "rights" parameter is mutually exclusive with the "advanced_rights" parameter. ONTAP translates rights into advanced_rights and this module is not idempotent when rights are used. Make sure to use C(advanced_rights) to maintain idempotency.  C(rights) can be used to discover the mapping to C(advanced_rights).

apply_to.description: Specifies where to apply the DACL or SACL entries. With SLAGs, ONTAP accepts the three suboptions to be set to true, but creates 2 ACLs. This module requires the 2 ACLs to be present to preserve idempotency. See also C(validate_changes).

advanced_rights.description: Specifies the advanced access right controlled by the ACE for the account specified.

ignore_paths.description: For each file or directory in the list, specifies that permissions on this file or directory cannot be replaced.

propagation_mode.description: Specifies how to propagate security settings to child subfolders and files. Defaults to propagate. This option is valid in create, but cannot modify.

validate_changes.description: ACLs may not be applied as expected. For instance, if Everyone is inherited will all permissions, additional users will be granted all permissions, regardless of the request. For this specific example, you can either delete the top level Everyone, or create a new ACL for Everyone at a lower level. When using C(rights), ONTAP translates them into C(advanced_rights) so the validation will always fail. Valid values are C(ignore), no checking; C(warn) to issue a warning; C(error) to fail the module. With SLAGS, ONTAP may split one ACL into two ACLs depending on the C(apply_to) settings.  To maintain idempotency, please provide 2 ACLs as input.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_firewall_policy.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Manage a firewall policy
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_firewall_policy.description: Configure firewall on an ONTAP node and manage firewall policy for an ONTAP SVM

state.description: Whether to set up a firewall policy or not

allow_list.description: A list of IPs and masks to use. The host bits of the IP addresses used in this list must be set to 0.

policy.description: A policy name for the firewall policy

service.description: The service to apply the policy to https and ssh are not supported starting with ONTAP 9.6 portmap is supported for ONTAP 9.4, 9.5 and 9.6 none is supported for ONTAP 9.8 onwards.

vserver.description: The Vserver to apply the policy to.

logging.description: enable logging for firewall on a node

node.description: The node to run the firewall configuration on

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_firmware_upgrade.short_description: NetApp ONTAP firmware upgrade for SP, shelf, ACP, and disk.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_firmware_upgrade.description: Update ONTAP service-prosessor firmware The recommend procedure is to 1. download the firmware package from the NetApp Support site 2. copy the package to a web server 3. download the package from the web server using this module Once a disk qualification, disk, shelf, or ACP firmware package is downloaded, ONTAP will automatically update the related resources in background. It may take some time to complete. For service processor, the update requires a node reboot to take effect.

state.description: Whether the specified ONTAP firmware should be upgraded or not.

node.description: Node on which the device is located. With REST, this parameter is ignored when package_url is provided but it is used with ZAPI. not required if package_url is present and force_disruptive_update is False. With REST, if this option is not given, the firmware will be downloaded on all nodes in the cluster and the resources will be updated in background on all nodes, except for service processor. with ZAPI, this parameter is used to specify the target node for firmware download. with ZAPI, if not specified firmware will be downloaded on all nodes in the cluster (using '*'). For service processor, the upgrade will happen automatically when each node is rebooted.

clear_logs.description: Clear logs on the device after update. Default value is true. Not used if force_disruptive_update is False. Not supported with REST when set to false.

package.description: Name of the package file containing the firmware to be installed. Not required when -baseline is true. Not used if force_disruptive_update is False. Not supported with REST.

package_url.description: URL of the package file containing the firmware to be downloaded. Once the package file is downloaded to a node, the firmware update will happen automatically in background. For SP, the upgrade will happen automatically when a node is rebooted. For SP, the upgrade will happen automatically if autoupdate is enabled (which is the recommended setting).

force_disruptive_update.description: If set to C(False), and URL is given, the upgrade is non disruptive. If URL is not given, no operation is performed. Do not set this to C(True), unless directed by NetApp Tech Support. It will force an update even if the resource is not ready for it, and can be disruptive. Not supported with REST when set to true.

shelf_module_fw.description: Shelf module firmware to be updated to. Not used if force_disruptive_update is False (ONTAP will automatically select the firmware) Not supported with REST.

disk_fw.description: disk firmware to be updated to. Not used if force_disruptive_update is False (ONTAP will automatically select the firmware) Not supported with REST.

update_type.description: Type of firmware update to be performed. Options include serial_full, serial_differential, network_full. Not used if force_disruptive_update is False (ONTAP will automatically select the firmware) Not supported with REST.

install_baseline_image.description: Install the version packaged with ONTAP if this parameter is set to true. Otherwise, package must be used to specify the package to install. Not used if force_disruptive_update is False (ONTAP will automatically select the firmware) Not supported with REST when set to true.

firmware_type.description: Type of firmware to be upgraded. Options include shelf, ACP, service-processor, and disk. For shelf firmware upgrade the operation is asynchronous, and therefore returns no errors that might occur during the download process. Shelf firmware upgrade is idempotent if shelf_module_fw is provided . disk firmware upgrade is idempotent if disk_fw is provided . With check mode, SP, ACP, disk, and shelf firmware upgrade is not idempotent. This operation will only update firmware on shelves/disk that do not have the latest firmware-revision. For normal operations, choose one of storage or service-processor. Type storage includes acp, shelf and disk and ONTAP will automatically determine what to do. With REST, the module does not validate that the package matches the firmware type.  ONTAP determines the type automatically. With REST, C(storage) downloads any firmware, including service-processor firmware. With REST, C(service-processor) unlocks SP reboot options.

fail_on_502_error.description: The firmware download may take time if the web server is slow and if there are many nodes in the cluster. ONTAP will break the ZAPI connection after 5 minutes with a 502 Bad Gateway error, even though the download is still happening. By default, this module ignores this error and assumes the download is progressing as ONTAP does not provide a way to check the status. When setting this option to true, the module will report 502 as an error. Not supported with REST when set to true.

rename_package.description: Rename the package. Only available if 'firmware_type' is 'service-processor'. Not supported with REST.

replace_package.description: Replace the local package. Only available if 'firmware_type' is 'service-processor'. Not supported with REST when set to false.

reboot_sp.description: Reboot service processor before downloading package. Only available if 'firmware_type' is 'service-processor'. Defaults to True if not set when 'firmware_type' is 'service-processor'. Set this explictly to true to avoid a warning, and to false to not reboot the SP. Rebooting the SP before download is strongly recommended.

reboot_sp_after_download.description: Reboot service processor after downloading package. Only available if 'firmware_type' is 'service-processor'.

server_username.description: username to authenticate with the firmware package server. Ignored with ZAPI.

server_password.description: password to authenticate with the firmware package server. Ignored with ZAPI.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_flexcache.description: Create/Modify/Delete FlexCache volume relationships. This module does not modify an existing FlexCache volume when using ZAPI. When using REST, a prepopulate can be started on an exising FlexCache volume. When using REST, the volume can be mounted or unmounted.  Set path to '' to unmount it. It is required the volume is mounted to prepopulate it. Some actions are also available through the na_ontap_volume. Cache volume can not be deleted in ZAPI.

state.description: Whether the specified relationship should exist or not.

origin_volume.description: Name of the origin volume for the FlexCache. Required for creation.

origin_vserver.description: Name of the origin vserver for the FlexCache. Required for creation.

origin_cluster.description: Name of the origin cluster for the FlexCache. Defaults to cluster associated with target vserver if absent. Not used for creation.

name.description: Name of the target volume for the FlexCache.

junction_path.description: Junction path of the cache volume.

auto_provision_as.description: Use this parameter to automatically select existing aggregates for volume provisioning.  Eg flexgroup Note that the fastest aggregate type with at least one aggregate on each node of the cluster will be selected. Ignored when using REST - omit aggr_list for automatic selection.

size_unit.description: The unit used to interpret the size parameter.

vserver.description: Name of the target vserver for the FlexCache. Note that hostname, username, password are intended for the target vserver.

aggr_list.description: List of aggregates to host target FlexCache volume.

aggr_list_multiplier.description: Aggregate list repeat count. REST - Number of FlexCache constituents per aggregate when the C(aggregates) field is mentioned.

force_unmount.description: Unmount FlexCache volume. Delete the junction path at which the volume is mounted before deleting the FlexCache relationship.

force_offline.description: Offline FlexCache volume before deleting the FlexCache relationship. The volume will be destroyed and data can be lost.

time_out.description: time to wait for flexcache creation or deletion in seconds if 0, the request is asynchronous default is set to 3 minutes

writeback.description: FlexCache Writeback. Requires ONTAP 9.12 or later and only supported with REST.

prepopulate.description: prepopulate FlexCache with data from origin volume. requires ONTAP 9.8 or later, and REST support. dir_paths must be set for this option to be effective.

relative_size.description: Only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.13.1 or later.

override_encryption.description: If set to true, a plaintext FlexCache volume for an encrypted origin volume is created. Only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.14.1 or later.

atime_scrub.description: Only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.14.1 or later.

cifs_change_notify_enabled.description: Specifies whether a CIFS change notification is enabled for the FlexCache volume. Only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.15.1 or later.

global_file_locking_enabled.description: Specifies whether or not a FlexCache volume has global file locking mode enabled. When global file locking mode is enabled, the 'is_disconnected_mode_off_for_locks' flag is always set to 'true'. Only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.9 or later.

guarantee_type.description: Specifies The type of space guarantee of this volume in the aggregate. A value of 'volume' reserves space on the aggregates for the entire volume. A value of 'none' reserves no space on the aggregates, meaning that writes can fail if an aggregate runs out of space. Only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.7 or later.

dr_cache.description: If set to true, a DR cache is created. Only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.9 or later.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fpolicy_ext_engine.short_description: NetApp ONTAP fPolicy external engine configuration.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fpolicy_ext_engine.description: Create, delete or modify fpolicy external engine.

state.description: Whether the fPolicy external engine is present or not

vserver.description: the name of the vserver to create the external engine on

name.description: Name of the external engine.

certificate_ca.description: Certificate authority name. No default value is set for this field.

certificate_common_name.description: FQDN or custom common name of certificate. No default value is set for this field.

certificate_serial.description: Serial number of certificate. No default value is set for this field.

extern_engine_type.description: External engine type. If the engine is asynchronous, no reply is sent from FPolicy servers. Default value set for this field is synchronous.

is_resiliency_enabled.description: Indicates if the resiliency with this engine is required. If set to true, the notifications will be stored in a path as resiliency_directory_path If it is false, the notifications will not be stored. Default value is false.

max_connection_retries.description: Number of times storage appliance will attempt to establish a broken connection to FPolicy server. Default value set for this field is 5.

max_server_reqs.description: Maximum number of outstanding screen requests that will be queued for an FPolicy Server. Default value set for this field is 50.

port.description: Port number of the FPolicy server application.

recv_buffer_size.description: Receive buffer size of connected socket for FPolicy Server. Default value set for this field is 256 kilobytes (256Kb).

resiliency_directory_path.description: Directory path under Vserver for storing file access notifications. File access notifications will be stored in a generated file during the outage time. The path is the full, user visible path relative to the Vserver root, and it might be crossing junction mount points.

secondary_servers.description: Secondary FPolicy servers. No default value is set for this field.

send_buffer_size.description: Send buffer size of connected socket for FPolicy Server. Default value set for this field is 256 kilobytes (256Kb).

ssl_option.description: SSL option for external communication. No default value is set for this field

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fpolicy_policy.short_description: NetApp ONTAP - Create, delete or modify an FPolicy policy.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fpolicy_policy.description: Create, delete or modify an FPolicy policy. Fpolicy scope must exist before running this module. FPolicy is a file access notification framework that enables an administrator to monitor file and directory access in storage configured for CIFS and NFS.

state.description: Whether the fPolicy policy should exist or not

vserver.description: the name of the vserver to create the policy on

name.description: Name of the policy.

allow_privileged_access.description: Specifies if privileged access should be given to FPolicy servers registered for the policy.

engine.description: Name of the Engine. External engines must be created prior to running this task.

is_mandatory.description: Specifies the action to take on a file access event in the case when all primary and secondary servers are down or no response is received from the FPolicy servers within a given timeout period. When True, file access events will be denied under these circumstances

is_passthrough_read_enabled.description: Specifies if passthrough-read should be allowed to FPolicy servers registered for the policy.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fpolicy_scope.short_description: NetApp ONTAP - Create, delete or modify an FPolicy policy scope configuration.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fpolicy_scope.description: Create, delete or modify an FPolicy policy scope.

state.description: Whether the FPolicy policy scope is present or not

vserver.description: the name of the vserver to create the scope on

name.description: Name of the policy.  The FPolicy policy must exist for the scope to be created.

check_extensions_on_directories.description: Indicates whether directory names are also subjected to extensions check, similar to file names. By default, the value is true if policy is configured with Native engine, false otherwise.

export_policies_to_exclude.description: Export Policies to exclude for file access monitoring. By default no export policy is selected.

export_policies_to_include.description: Export policies to include for file access monitoring. By default no export policy is selected.

file_extensions_to_exclude.description: File extensions excluded for screening. By default no file extension is selected.

file_extensions_to_include.description: File extensions included for screening. By default no file extension is selected.

is_monitoring_of_objects_with_no_extensi.description: Indicates whether monitoring of objects with no extension is required. By default, the value is false.

shares_to_exclude.description: Shares to exclude for file access monitoring. By default no share is selected.

shares_to_include.description: Shares to include for file access monitoring. By default no share is selected.

volumes_to_exclude.description: Volumes that are inactive for the file policy. The list can include items which are regular expressions, such as 'vol*' or 'user?'. Note that if a policy has both an exclude list and an include list, the include list is ignored by the filer when processing user requests. By default no volume is selected.

volumes_to_include.description: Volumes that are active for the file policy. The list can include items which are regular expressions, such as 'vol*' or 'user?'. By default no volume is selected.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_fpolicy_status.short_description: NetApp ONTAP - Enables or disables the specified fPolicy policy

state.description: Whether the fPolicy policy is enabled or disabled.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to enable fPolicy on.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_igroup.short_description: NetApp ONTAP iSCSI or FC igroup configuration
