netapp.ontap.na_ontap_igroup.description: Create/Delete/Rename Igroups and Modify initiators belonging to an igroup

state.description: Whether the specified Igroup should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the igroup to manage.

initiator_group_type.description: Type of the initiator group. Required when C(state=present).

from_name.description: Name of igroup to rename to name.

os_type.description: OS type of the initiators within the group.

igroups.description: List of igroups to be mapped to the igroup. For a modify operation, this list replaces the existing igroups, or existing initiators. This module does not add or remove specific igroup(s) in an igroup. Mutually exclusive with initiator_names (initiators) and initiator_objects. Requires ONTAP 9.9 or newer.

initiator_names.description: List of initiators to be mapped to the igroup. WWPN, WWPN Alias, or iSCSI name of Initiator to add or remove. For a modify operation, this list replaces the existing initiators, or existing igroups. This module does not add or remove specific initiator(s) in an igroup. Mutually exclusive with igroups and initiator_objects. This serves the same purpose as initiator_objects, but without the comment suboption.

initiator_objects.description: List of initiators to be mapped to the igroup, with an optional comment field. WWPN, WWPN Alias, or iSCSI name of Initiator to add or remove. For a modify operation, this list replaces the existing initiators, or existing igroups. This module does not add or remove specific initiator(s) in an igroup. Mutually exclusive with initiator_names (initiators) and igroups. Requires ONTAP 9.9 with REST support.

bind_portset.description: Name of a current portset to bind to the newly created igroup.

force_remove_initiator.description: Forcibly remove the initiator even if there are existing LUNs mapped to this initiator group. This parameter should be used with caution.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

state.description: Whether the specified initiator should exist or not in an igroup.

initiator_group.description: Name of the initiator group to which the initiator belongs.

force_remove.description: Forcibly remove the initiators even if there are existing LUNs mapped to the initiator group.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_info.description: This module allows you to gather various information about ONTAP configuration

state.description: deprecated as of 21.1.0. this option was ignored and continues to be ignored.

vserver.description: If present, 'vserver tunneling' will limit the output to the vserver scope. Note that not all subsets are supported on a vserver, and 'all' will trigger an error.

gather_subset.description: When supplied, this argument will restrict the information collected to a given subset.  Possible values for this argument include active_directory_account_info aggregate_info aggr_efficiency_info autosupport_check_info cifs_options_info cifs_server_info cifs_share_info cifs_vserver_security_info cluster_identity_info cluster_image_info cluster_log_forwarding_info cluster_node_info cluster_peer_info cluster_switch_info clock_info disk_info env_sensors_info event_notification_destination_info event_notification_info export_policy_info export_rule_info fcp_adapter_info fcp_alias_info fcp_service_info igroup_info iscsi_service_info job_schedule_cron_info kerberos_realm_info ldap_client ldap_config license_info lun_info lun_map_info metrocluster_check_info metrocluster_info metrocluster_node_info net_dev_discovery_info net_dns_info net_failover_group_info net_firewall_info net_ifgrp_info net_interface_info net_interface_service_policy_info net_ipspaces_info net_port_info net_port_broadcast_domain_info net_routes_info net_vlan_info nfs_info ntfs_dacl_info ntfs_sd_info ntp_server_info nvme_info nvme_interface_info nvme_namespace_info nvme_subsystem_info ontap_system_version ontap_version ontapi_version qos_adaptive_policy_info qos_policy_info qtree_info quota_policy_info quota_report_info role_info security_key_manager_key_info security_login_account_info security_login_role_config_info security_login_role_info service_processor_info service_processor_network_info shelf_info sis_info sis_policy_info snapmirror_info snapmirror_destination_info snapmirror_policy_info snapshot_info snapshot_policy_info storage_failover_info storage_bridge_info subsys_health_info sysconfig_info sys_cluster_alerts volume_info volume_space_info vscan_info vscan_status_info vscan_scanner_pool_info vscan_connection_status_all_info vscan_connection_extended_stats_info vserver_info vserver_login_banner_info vserver_motd_info vserver_nfs_info vserver_peer_info Can specify a list of values to include a larger subset. Values can also be used with an initial C(!) to specify that a specific subset should not be collected. nvme is supported with ONTAP 9.4 onwards. use "help" to get a list of supported information for your system. with lun_info, serial_hex and naa_id are computed when serial_number is present.

max_records.description: Maximum number of records returned in a single ZAPI call. Valid range is [1..2^32-1]. This parameter controls internal behavior of this module.

summary.description: Boolean flag to control return all attributes of the module info or only the names. If true, only names are returned.

desired_attributes.description: Advanced feature requiring to understand ZAPI internals. Allows to request a specific attribute that is not returned by default, or to limit the returned attributes. A dictionary for the zapi desired-attributes element. An XML tag I(<tag>value</tag>) is a dictionary with tag as the key. Value can be another dictionary, a list of dictionaries, a string, or nothing. eg I(<tag/>) is represented as I(tag:) Only a single subset can be called at a time if this option is set. It is the caller responsibity to make sure key attributes are present in the right position. The module will error out if any key attribute is missing.

query.description: Advanced feature requiring to understand ZAPI internals. Allows to specify which objects to return. A dictionary for the zapi query element. An XML tag I(<tag>value</tag>) is a dictionary with tag as the key. Value can be another dictionary, a list of dictionaries, a string, or nothing. eg I(<tag/>) is represented as I(tag:) Only a single subset can be called at a time if this option is set.

use_native_zapi_tags.description: By default, I(-) in the returned dictionary keys are translated to I(_). If set to true, the translation is disabled.

continue_on_error.description: By default, this module fails on the first error. This option allows to provide a list of errors that are not failing the module. Errors in the list are reported in the output, under the related info element, as an "error" entry. Possible values are always, never, missing_vserver_api_error, rpc_error, other_error. missing_vserver_api_error - most likely the API is available at cluster level but not vserver level. rpc_error - some queries are failing because the node cannot reach another node in the cluster. key_error - a query is failing because the returned data does not contain an expected key. for key errors, make sure to report this in Discord.  It may be a change in a new ONTAP version. other_error - anything not in the above list. always will continue on any error, never will fail on any error, they cannot be used with any other keyword.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_interface.description: Creating / deleting and modifying the LIF.

state.description: Whether the specified interface should exist or not.

home_node.description: Specifies the LIF's home node. By default, the first node from the cluster is considered as home node.

current_node.description: Specifies the LIF's current node. By default, this is home_node

home_port.description: Specifies the LIF's home port. Requires ONTAP 9.8 or later with FC interfaces when using REST. With REST, at least one of home_port, home_node, or broadcast_domain is required to create IP interfaces. With REST, either home_port or current_port is required to create FC interfaces. With ZAPI, home_port is required to create IP and FC interfaces. home_port and broadcast_domain are mutually exclusive (REST and IP interfaces).

role.description: Specifies the role of the LIF. When setting role as "intercluster" or "cluster", setting protocol is not supported. When creating a "cluster" role, the node name will appear as the prefix in the name of LIF. For example, if the specified name is clif and node name is node1, the LIF name appears in the ONTAP as node1_clif. Possible values are 'undef', 'cluster', 'data', 'node-mgmt', 'intercluster', 'cluster-mgmt'. Required when C(state=present) unless service_policy is present and ONTAP version is 9.8 or better. This option is deprecated in REST. With REST, the module tries to derive a service_policy and may error out.

address.description: Specifies the LIF's IP address. ZAPI - Required when C(state=present) and is_ipv4_link_local if false and subnet_name is not set. REST - Required when C(state=present) and C(interface_type) is IP.

netmask.description: Specifies the LIF's netmask. ZAPI - Required when C(state=present) and is_ipv4_link_local if false and subnet_name is not set. REST - Required when C(state=present) and C(interface_type) is IP.

is_ipv4_link_local.description: Specifies the LIF's are to acquire a ipv4 link local address. Use case for this is when creating Cluster LIFs to allow for auto assignment of ipv4 link local address. Not supported in REST

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use. Required with ZAPI. Required with REST for FC interfaces (data vservers). Required with REST for SVM-scoped IP interfaces (data vservers). Invalid with REST for cluster-scoped IP interfaces. To help with transition from ZAPI to REST, vserver is ignored when the role is set to 'cluster', 'node-mgmt', 'intercluster', 'cluster-mgmt'. Remove this option to suppress the warning.

firewall_policy.description: Specifies the firewall policy for the LIF. This option is deprecated in REST. With REST, the module tries to derive a service_policy and may error out.

failover_policy.description: Specifies the failover policy for the LIF. When using REST, this values are mapped to 'home_port_only', 'default', 'home_node_only', 'sfo_partners_only', 'broadcast_domain_only'.

failover_scope.description: Specifies the failover scope for the LIF. REST only, and only for IP interfaces.  Not supported for FC interfaces.

failover_group.description: Specifies the failover group for the LIF. Not supported with REST.

subnet_name.description: Subnet where the IP interface address is allocated from. If the option is not used, the IP address and netmask need to be provided. With REST, ONTAP 9.11.1 or later is required. With REST, ipspace must be set.

fail_if_subnet_conflicts.description: Creating or updating an IP Interface fails if the specified IP address falls within the address range of a named subnet. Set this value to false to use the specified IP address and to assign the subnet owning that address to the interface. This option is only supported with REST and requires ONTAP 9.11.1 or later.

admin_status.description: Specifies the administrative status of the LIF.

is_auto_revert.description: If true, data LIF will revert to its home node under certain circumstances such as startup, and load balancing migration capability is disabled automatically

force_subnet_association.description: Set this to true to acquire the address from the named subnet and assign the subnet to the LIF. not supported with REST.

protocols.description: Specifies the list of data protocols configured on the LIF. By default, the values in this element are nfs, cifs and fcache. Other supported protocols are iscsi and fcp. A LIF can be configured to not support any data protocols by specifying 'none'. Protocol values of none, iscsi, fc-nvme or fcp can't be combined with any other data protocol(s). address, netmask and firewall_policy parameters are not supported for 'fc-nvme' option. This option is ignored with REST, though it can be used to derive C(interface_type) or C(data_protocol).

data_protocol.description: The data protocol for which the FC interface is configured. Ignored with ZAPI or for IP interfaces. Required to create a FC type interface.

dns_domain_name.description: Specifies the unique, fully qualified domain name of the DNS zone of this LIF. Supported from ONTAP 9.9.0 or later in REST. Not supported for FC interfaces.

listen_for_dns_query.description: If True, this IP address will listen for DNS queries for the dnszone specified. Not supported with REST.

is_dns_update_enabled.description: Specifies if DNS update is enabled for this LIF. Dynamic updates will be sent for this LIF if updates are enabled at Vserver level. Supported from ONTAP 9.9.1 or later in REST. Not supported for FC interfaces.

service_policy.description: Starting with ONTAP 9.5, you can configure LIF service policies to identify a single service or a list of services that will use a LIF. In ONTAP 9.5, you can assign service policies only for LIFs in the admin SVM. In ONTAP 9.6, you can additionally assign service policies for LIFs in the data SVMs. When you specify a service policy for a LIF, you need not specify the data protocol and role for the LIF. NOTE that role is still required because of a ZAPI issue.  This limitation is removed in ONTAP 9.8. Creating LIFs by specifying the role and data protocols is also supported.

from_name.description: name of the interface to be renamed

interface_type.description: type of the interface. IP is assumed if address or netmask are present. IP interfaces includes cluster, intercluster, management, and NFS, CIFS, iSCSI interfaces. FC interfaces includes FCP and NVME-FC interfaces. ignored with ZAPI. required with REST, but maybe derived from deprecated options like C(role), C(protocols), and C(firewall_policy).

ipspace.description: IPspace name is required with REST for cluster-scoped interfaces.  It is optional with SVM scope. ignored with ZAPI. ignored for FC interface.

broadcast_domain.description: broadcast_domain name can be used to specify the location on an IP interface with REST, as an alternative to node or port. only used when creating an IP interface to select a node, ignored if the interface already exists. if the broadcast domain is not found, make sure to check the ipspace value. home_port and broadcast_domain are mutually exclusive.  home_node may or may not be present. not supported for FC interface. ignored with ZAPI.

ignore_zapi_options.description: ignore unsupported options that should not be relevant. ignored with ZAPI.

probe_port.description: Probe port for Cloud load balancer - only valid in the Azure environment. Not supported with ZAPI or with FC interfaces. Requires ONTAP 9.10.1 or later.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ipspace.description: Manage an ipspace for an Ontap Cluster

state.description: Whether the specified ipspace should exist or not

name.description: The name of the ipspace to manage

from_name.description: Name of the existing ipspace to be renamed to name

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_iscsi.description: create, delete, start, stop iSCSI service on SVM.

state.description: Whether the service should be present or deleted.

service_state.description: Whether the specified service should running.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

target_alias.description: The iSCSI target alias of the iSCSI service. The target alias can contain one (1) to 128 characters and feature any printable character except space (" "). A PATCH request with an empty alias ("") clears the alias. This option is REST only.

state.description: Whether the specified initiator should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

initiator.description: Specifies the name of the initiator.

address_ranges.description: May be a single IPv4 or IPv6 address or a range containing a startaddress and an end address. The start and end addresses themselves are included in the range. If not present, the initiator is allowed to log in from any IP address.

inbound_username.description: Inbound CHAP username. Required for CHAP. A null username is not allowed.

inbound_password.description: Inbound CHAP user password. Can not be modified. If want to change password, delete and re-create the initiator.

outbound_password.description: Outbound CHAP user password. Can not be modified. If want to change password, delete and re-create the initiator.

state.description: Whether the specified job schedule should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the job-schedule to manage.

job_minutes.description: The minute(s) of each hour when the job should be run. Job Manager cron scheduling minute. C(-1) represents all minutes. Range is [-1..59] Required for create.

job_hours.description: The hour(s) of the day when the job should be run. Job Manager cron scheduling hour. C(-1) represents all hours. Range is [-1..23]

job_months.description: The month(s) when the job should be run. Job Manager cron scheduling month. C(-1) represents all months. Range is [-1..12], 0 and 12 may or may not be supported, see C(month_offset)

job_days_of_month.description: The day(s) of the month when the job should be run. Job Manager cron scheduling day of month. C(-1) represents all days of a month from 1 to 31. Range is [-1..31]

job_days_of_week.description: The day(s) in the week when the job should be run. Job Manager cron scheduling day of week. Zero represents Sunday. C(-1) represents all days of a week. Range is [-1..6]

month_offset.description: whether January starts at 0 or 1.  By default, ZAPI is using a 0..11 range, while REST is using 1..12. default to 0 when using ZAPI, and to 1 when using REST. when set to 0, a value of 12 or higher is rejected. when set to 1, a value of 0 or of 13 or higher is rejected.

cluster.description: Defaults to local cluster. In a MetroCluster configuration, user-created schedules owned by the local cluster are replicated to the partner cluster. Likewise, user-created schedules owned by the partner cluster are replicated to the local cluster. Normally, only schedules owned by the local cluster can be created, modified, and deleted on the local cluster. However, when a MetroCluster configuration is in switchover, the cluster in switchover state can create, modify, and delete schedules owned by the partner cluster.

vserver.description: name of the vserver.

interval.description: The interval at which the job should be run. This is specified in an ISO-8601 duration formatted string. This parameter does not work with cron jobs.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_kerberos_interface.short_description: NetApp ONTAP module to modify kerberos interface.

state.description: Modify kerberos interface, only present is supported.

interface_name.description: Specifies the name of the logical interface associated with the NFS Kerberos configuration you want to modify.

vserver.description: Specifies the Vserver associated with the NFS Kerberos configuration you want to modify.

enabled.description: Specifies whether to enable or disable Kerberos for NFS on the specified Vserver and logical interface. C(service_principal_name) is required when try to enable kerberos.

keytab_uri.description: Specifies loading a keytab file from the specified URI. This value must be in the form of "(ftp|http|https)://(hostname|IPv4 Address|'['IPv6 Address']')...".

machine_account.description: Specifies the machine account to create in Active Directory. Requires ONTAP 9.12.1 or later.

organizational_unit.description: Specifies the organizational unit (OU) under which the Microsoft Active Directory server account will be created when you enable Kerberos using a realm for Microsoft KDC

service_principal_name.description: Specifies the service principal name (SPN) of the Kerberos configuration you want to modify. This value must be in the form nfs/host_name@REALM. host_name is the fully qualified host name of the Kerberos server, nfs is the service, and REALM is the name of the Kerberos realm. Specify Kerberos realm names in uppercase.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_kerberos_realm.short_description: NetApp ONTAP vserver nfs kerberos realm
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_kerberos_realm.description: Create, modify or delete vserver kerberos realm configuration

state.description: Whether the Kerberos realm is present or absent.

vserver.description: vserver/svm with kerberos realm configured

kdc_vendor.description: The vendor of the Key Distribution Centre (KDC) server Required if I(state=present)

kdc_ip.description: IP address of the Key Distribution Centre (KDC) server Required if I(state=present)

kdc_port.description: TCP port on the KDC to be used for Kerberos communication. The default for this parameter is 88.

clock_skew.description: The clock skew in minutes is the tolerance for accepting tickets with time stamps that do not exactly match the host's system clock. The default for this parameter is '5' minutes. Supported from ONTAP 9.13.1 in REST.

admin_server_ip.description: IP address of the host where the Kerberos administration daemon is running. This is usually the master KDC. If this parameter is omitted, the address specified in kdc_ip is used. Supported from ONTAP 9.13.1 in REST.

admin_server_port.description: The TCP port on the Kerberos administration server where the Kerberos administration service is running. The default for this parmater is '749'. Supported from ONTAP 9.13.1 in REST.

pw_server_ip.description: IP address of the host where the Kerberos password-changing server is running. Typically, this is the same as the host indicated in the adminserver-ip. If this parameter is omitted, the IP address in kdc-ip is used. Supported from ONTAP 9.13.1 in REST.

pw_server_port.description: The TCP port on the Kerberos password-changing server where the Kerberos password-changing service is running. The default for this parameter is '464'. Supported from ONTAP 9.13.1 in REST.

ad_server_ip.description: IP Address of the Active Directory Domain Controller (DC). This is a mandatory parameter if the kdc-vendor is 'microsoft'.

ad_server_name.description: Host name of the Active Directory Domain Controller (DC). This is a mandatory parameter if the kdc-vendor is 'microsoft'.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ldap.description: Create, modify or delete LDAP on NetApp ONTAP SVM/vserver

state.description: Whether the LDAP is present or not.

vserver.description: vserver/svm configured to use LDAP

name.description: The name of LDAP client configuration

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ldap_client.description: Create, modify or delete LDAP client on NetApp ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified LDAP client configuration exist or not.

vserver.description: vserver/svm that holds LDAP client configuration.

name.description: The name of LDAP client configuration. Supported only in ZAPI. Required with ZAPI.

servers.description: Comma separated list of LDAP servers. FQDN's or IP addreses. servers or ad_domain is required if I(state=present). Mutually exclusive with preferred_ad_servers and ad_domain.

schema.description: LDAP schema. Required if I(state=present). default schemas - 'AD-IDMU', 'AD-SFU', 'MS-AD-BIS', 'RFC-2307'. custom schemas are allowed as well.

ad_domain.description: Active Directory Domain Name. servers or ad_domain is required if I(state=present). Mutually exclusive with servers.

bind_as_cifs_server.description: The cluster uses the CIFS server's credentials to bind to the LDAP server.

preferred_ad_servers.description: Preferred Active Directory (AD) Domain Controllers. Mutually exclusive with servers.

ldaps_enabled.description: Specifies whether or not LDAPS is enabled.

skip_config_validation.description: Indicates whether or not the validation for the specified LDAP configuration is disabled. By default, errors are reported with REST when server names cannot be resolved for instance. Requires ONTAP 9.9 or later. This is ignored with ZAPI.

group_dn.description: Specifies the group Distinguished Name (DN) that is used as the starting point in the LDAP directory tree for group lookups.

user_dn.description: Specifies the user Distinguished Name (DN) that is used as the starting point in the LDAP directory tree for user lookups.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_license.short_description: NetApp ONTAP protocol and feature license packages
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_license.description: Add or remove license packages on NetApp ONTAP. Note that the module is asymmetrical. It requires license codes to add packages and the package name is not visible. It requires package names and as serial number to remove packages.

state.description: Whether the specified license packages should be installed or removed.

remove_unused.description: Remove license packages that have no controller affiliation in the cluster. Not supported with REST.

remove_expired.description: Remove license packages that have expired in the cluster. Not supported with REST.

serial_number.description: Serial number of the node or cluster associated with the license package. This parameter is required when removing a license package. With REST, '*' is accepted and matches any serial number.

license_names.description: List of license package names to remove.

license_codes.description: List of license codes to be installed.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_local_hosts.description: Create or delete or modify local hosts in ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified local hosts should exist or not.

owner.description: Name of the data SVM or cluster.

host.description: Canonical hostname. minimum length is 1 and maximum length is 255.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_log_forward.description: Create, delete or modify the log forward configuration

state.description: Whether the log forward configuration should exist or not

destination.description: Destination address that the log messages will be forwarded to. Can be a hostname or IP address.

port.description: The destination port used to forward the message.

facility.description: Facility code used to indicate the type of software that generated the message.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_login_messages.short_description: Setup login banner and message of the day
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_login_messages.description: This module allows you to manipulate login banner and motd for a vserver

vserver.description: The name of the SVM login messages should be set for. With ZAPI, this option is required.  This a cluster or data SVM. With REST, this is a data SVM. With REST, cluster scope is assumed when this option is absent.

motd_message.description: MOTD Text message. message is deprecated and will be removed to avoid a conflict with an Ansible internal variable.

show_cluster_motd.description: Set to I(false) if Cluster-level Message of the Day should not be shown

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_lun.description: Create, destroy, resize LUNs on NetApp ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified LUN should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the LUN to manage. Or LUN group name (volume name) when san_application_template is used.

from_name.description: The name of the LUN to be renamed.

flexvol_name.description: The name of the FlexVol the LUN should exist on. Required if san_application_template is not present. Not allowed if san_application_template is present. Not supported for ASA r2 system.

qtree_name.description: Specifies the name of the Qtree that contains the new LUN. Not allowed if san_application_template is present. Only supported with REST. Qtrees are not supported with ASA r2 system.

size.description: The size of the LUN in C(size_unit). Required when creating a single LUN if application template is not used.

size_unit.description: The unit used to interpret the size parameter.

comment.description: Optional descriptive comment for the LUN.

force_resize.description: Forcibly reduce the size. This is required for reducing the size of the LUN to avoid accidentally reducing the LUN size. Not supported with REST.

force_remove.description: If "true", override checks that prevent a LUN from being destroyed if it is online and mapped. If "false", destroying an online and mapped LUN will fail.

force_remove_fenced.description: If "true", override checks that prevent a LUN from being destroyed while it is fenced. If "false", attempting to destroy a fenced LUN will fail. The default if not specified is "false". This field is available in Data ONTAP 8.2 and later. Not supported with REST.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

os_type.description: The os type for the LUN.

qos_policy_group.description: The QoS policy group to be set on the LUN. With REST, qos_policy_group and qos_adaptive_policy_group are handled as QOS policy.

qos_adaptive_policy_group.description: The adaptive QoS policy group to be set on the LUN. Defines measurable service level objectives (SLOs) and service level agreements (SLAs) that adjust based on the LUN's allocated space or used space. Requires ONTAP 9.4 or later. With REST, qos_policy_group and qos_adaptive_policy_group are handled as QOS policy.

space_reserve.description: This can be set to "false" which will create a LUN without any space being reserved. Not supported for ASA r2 system. All LUNs are provisioned without a space reservation.

space_allocation.description: This enables support for the SCSI Thin Provisioning features.  If the Host and file system do not support this do not enable it. Not supported for ASA r2 system. All LUNs are provisioned with SCSI thin provisioning enabled.

use_exact_size.description: This can be set to "false" which will round the LUN >= 450g.

san_application_template.description: additional options when using the application/applications REST API to create LUNs. the module is using ZAPI by default, and switches to REST if san_application_template is present. create one or more LUNs (and the associated volume as needed). operations at the LUN level are supported, they require to know the LUN short name. this requires ONTAP 9.8 or higher. The module partially supports ONTAP 9.7 for create and delete operations, but not for modify (API limitations). Not supported with ASA r2 system.

provisioning_options.description: Options that are applied to create operation. Only supported with REST, requires ONTAP 9.16.1 or later.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

state.description: Whether the specified LUN should exist or not.

destination_vserver.description: the name of the Vserver that will host the new LUN.

destination_path.description: Specifies the full path to the new LUN.

source_path.description: Specifies the full path to the source LUN.

source_vserver.description: Specifies the name of the vserver hosting the LUN to be copied. If not provided, C(destination_vserver) value is set as default. with REST, this option value must match C(destination_vserver) when present.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_lun_map.description: Map and unmap LUNs on NetApp ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified LUN should exist or not.

initiator_group_name.description: Initiator group to map to the given LUN.

path.description: Path of the LUN. For ASA R2 systems, The path should match the format <name>[@<snapshot-name>].

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

lun_id.description: LUN ID assigned for the map.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_lun_map_reporting_.short_description: NetApp ONTAP LUN maps reporting nodes
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_lun_map_reporting_.description: Add and Remove LUN map reporting nodes.

state.description: Whether to add or remove reporting nodes

initiator_group_name.description: Initiator group to map to the given LUN.

path.description: Path of the LUN. For ASA R2 systems, The path should match the format <name>[@<snapshot-name>].

vserver.description: The name of the vserver owning the LUN.

nodes.description: List of reporting nodes to add or remove

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_mav_approval_group.short_description: NetApp ONTAP multi-admin verification (MAV) approval group

state.description: Specifies whether to create/modify or delete the specified approval group.

name.description: Specifies the name of an approval group.

email.description: Specifies the list of email addresses that are notified when a request is created, approved, vetoed, or executed.

approvers.description: Specifies the list of ONTAP users that are part of the approval group.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_mav_config.short_description: NetApp ONTAP multi-admin verification (MAV) global setting
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_mav_config.description: Enable/disable multi-admin verification (MAV) configuration. Multi-admin verification is not required to enable the feature. However, it is required to disable the feature. Multi-admin verification is required for any modification to be done while multi-admin approval is enabled. Until the pending request gets approved, request info is returned in the module output. Once approved the module needs to be run again to apply the intended updates.

state.description: Modify MAV global setting, only present is supported.

enabled.description: Specifies whether multi-admin approval is currently configured or not. By default, the feature is disabled and the value is set to false.

required_approvers.description: Specifies the required number of approvers to approve the request which is inherited by the rule if required-approvers is not provided for the rule. The default and minimum number of required approvers is 1.

approval_groups.description: Specifies the list of global approval groups which are inherited by the rule if the approval-groups is not provided for the rule. The default value is an empty list.

approval_expiry.description: Specifies the time, in ISO-8601 duration format, that the approvers have after a new execution request is submitted to approve or disapprove the request before the request expires. The default value is one hour (PT1H), the minimum supported value is one second (PT1S), and the maximum supported value is 14 days (P14D).

execution_expiry.description: Specifies the time, in ISO-8601 duration format, that the authorized users have after a request is approved to execute the requested operation before the request expires. The default value is one hour (PT1H), the minimum supported value is one second (PT1S), and the maximum supported value is 14 days (P14D).

state.description: Specifies whether to create/modify or delete the specified rule.

operation.description: Specifies the command that requires one or more approvals.

query.description: Specifies the query information which is applied to the subset of objects of ONTAP operation of the rule.

required_approvers.description: Specifies the number of required approvers, excluding the user that made the request. The default and minimum number of required approvers is 1.

approval_groups.description: Specifies the list of approval groups that are allowed to approve requests for rules that don't have approval groups.

approval_expiry.description: Specifies the time, in ISO-8601 duration format, that the approvers have after a new execution request is submitted to approve or disapprove the request before the request expires. The default value is one hour (PT1H), the minimum supported value is one second (PT1S), and the maximum supported value is 14 days (P14D).

execution_expiry.description: Specifies the time, in ISO-8601 duration format, that the authorized users have after a request is approved to execute the requested operation before the request expires. The default value is one hour (PT1H), the minimum supported value is one second (PT1S), and the maximum supported value is 14 days (P14D).

auto_request_create.description: When true, ONTAP automatically creates a request for any failed operation where there is no matching pending request. Defaults to True.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_mcc_mediator.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Add and Remove MetroCluster Mediator
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_mcc_mediator.description: Add and remove ONTAP MCC Mediator

state.description: Whether MCCIP Mediator is present or not.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_metrocluster.short_description: NetApp ONTAP set up a MetroCluster

state.description: Present to set up a MetroCluster

partner_cluster_name.description: The name of the partner Cluster

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_metrocluster_dr_gr.short_description: NetApp ONTAP manage MetroCluster DR Group
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_metrocluster_dr_gr.description: Create/Delete MetroCluster DR Group Create only supports MCC IP Delete supports both MCC IP and MCC FC

state.description: add or remove DR groups

partner_cluster_name.description: The name of the partner cluster

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_motd.description: This module allows you to manipulate motd for a vserver It also allows to manipulate motd at the cluster level by using the cluster vserver (cserver)

state.description: If C(state=present) sets MOTD given in I(message) C(state=absent) removes it.

motd_message.description: MOTD Text message. message is deprecated and will be removed to avoid a conflict with an Ansible internal variable.

vserver.description: The name of the SVM motd should be set for.

show_cluster_motd.description: Set to I(false) if Cluster-level Message of the Day should not be shown

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_name_mappings.description: Create/Delete/Modify name mappings for an SVM on ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified name mappings should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

client_match.description: Client workstation IP Address which is matched when searching for the pattern. Example '10.254.101.111/28' Client match value can be in any of the following formats, - As an IPv4 address with a subnet mask expressed as a number of bits; for instance, 10.1.12.0/24 - As an IPv6 address with a subnet mask expressed as a number of bits; for instance, fd20:8b1e:b255:4071::/64 - As an IPv4 address with a network mask; for instance, 10.1.16.0/255.255.255.0 - As a hostname

direction.description: Direction in which the name mapping is applied. The possible values are, krb_unix - Kerberos principal name to UNIX user name win_unix - Windows user name to UNIX user name unix_win - UNIX user name to Windows user name mapping s3_unix - S3 user name to UNIX user name mapping s3_win - S3 user name to Windows user name mapping s3_unix and s3_win requires ONTAP 9.12.1 or later.

index.description: Position in the list of name mappings. Minimum value is 1 and maximum is 2147483647.

pattern.description: Pattern used to match the name while searching for a name that can be used as a replacement. The pattern is a UNIX-style regular expression. Regular expressions are case-insensitive when mapping from Windows to UNIX, and they are case-sensitive for mappings from Kerberos to UNIX and UNIX to Windows. Minimum length is 1 and maximum length is 256. Pattern should be unique for each index of vserver. Example ENGCIFS_AD_USER.

replacement.description: The name that is used as a replacement, if the pattern associated with this entry matches. Minimum length is 1 and maximum length is 256. Example unix_user1.

from_index.description: If no entry with index is found, it is created by reindexing the entry for from_index. If no entry is found for index and from_index, an error is reported. Minimum value is 1 and maximum is 2147483647. Requires ONTAP version 9.7 or later.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_name_service_switc.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Manage name service switch
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_name_service_switc.description: Create/Delete/Modify Name Service Switch. Deleting name service switch not supported in REST.

state.description: Whether the specified ns-switch should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

sources.description: Type of sources. Possible values include files,dns,ldap,nis.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver.

data_port_range.description: Data port range. Modification not supported for data Vservers.

dump_detailed_stats.description: Enable logging of VM stats for dump.

restore_vm_cache_size.description: Restore VM file cache size. Value range [4-1024]

ndmp_user.description: The name of the NDMP user. This field cannot be specified in a PATCH method.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_net_ifgrp.short_description: NetApp Ontap modify network interface group
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_net_ifgrp.description: Create, modify ports, destroy the network interface group

state.description: Whether the specified network interface group should exist or not.

distribution_function.description: Specifies the traffic distribution function for the ifgrp.

name.description: Specifies the interface group name. Not supported with REST, use C(ports) or C(from_lag_ports). LAG names are automatically created in REST and returned in module output. Required with ZAPI.

mode.description: Specifies the link policy for the ifgrp.

ports.description: List of expected ports to be present in the interface group. If a port is present in this list, but not on the target, it will be added. If a port is not in the list, but present on the target, it will be removed. Make sure the list contains all ports you want to see on the target. With REST, ports in this list are used to find the current LAG port. If LAG is not found or only partial port matches, then C(from_lag_port) are used to get the current LAG. With REST, when C(state=absent) is set, all of the ports in ifgrp should be provided to delete it. Example C(ports=['e0c','e0a']) will delete ifgrp that has ports C(['e0c','e0a']).

from_lag_ports.description: Only supported with REST and is ignored with ZAPI. Specify all the ports to find current LAG port. Ignored if LAG found with exact match of C(ports). Example if current LAG has ports C(['e0c','e0d']) and C(ports=['e0c','e0d']), then from_lag_ports will be ignored. If LAG not found with C(ports), then ports in this list are used to find the current LAG. Ports in this list are used only for finding current LAG, provide exact match of all the ports in the current LAG. Ignored when C(state=absent).

broadcast_domain.description: Specify the broadcast_domain name. Only supported with REST and is ignored with ZAPI. Required with ONTAP 9.6 and 9.7, but optional with 9.8 or later.

ipspace.description: Specify the ipspace for the broadcast domain. Only supported with REST and is ignored with ZAPI. Required with ONTAP 9.6 and 9.7, but optional with 9.8 or later.

state.description: Whether the specified net port should exist or not.

mtu.description: Specifies the maximum transmission unit (MTU) reported by the port. Not supported with REST.

autonegotiate_admin.description: Enables or disables Ethernet auto-negotiation of speed, duplex and flow control. Not supported with REST.

duplex_admin.description: Specifies the user preferred duplex setting of the port. Valid values auto, half, full Not supported with REST.

speed_admin.description: Specifies the user preferred speed setting of the port. Not supported with REST.

flowcontrol_admin.description: Specifies the user preferred flow control setting of the port. Supported with REST for 9.16.1 and later.

ipspace.description: Specifies the port's associated IPspace name. The 'Cluster' ipspace is reserved for cluster ports. use netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ports to modify ipspace with REST.

state.description: Whether you want to create or delete a network route.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver. Required when using ZAPI. When using REST, omit this parameter for cluster scoped routes, or set it to NULL.

destination.description: Specify the route destination. Example 10.7.125.5/20, fd20:13::/64.

gateway.description: Specify the route gateway. Example 10.7.125.1, fd20:13::1.

metric.description: Specify the route metric.  If this field is not provided, ONTAP will default to 20. Supported from ONTAP 9.11.0 in REST. With REST, trying to modify destination or gateway will also reset metric to 20 in ONTAP 9.10.1 or earlier.

from_destination.description: Specify the route destination that should be changed.

from_gateway.description: Specify the route gateway that should be changed.

from_metric.description: Specify the route metric that should be changed. This parameter is ignored, as the value is read from ONTAP. Not supported with REST, ignored with ZAPI.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_net_subnet.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Create, delete, modify network subnets.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_net_subnet.description: Create, modify, destroy the network subnet

state.description: Whether the specified network interface group should exist or not.

broadcast_domain.description: Specify the required broadcast_domain name for the subnet. A broadcast domain can not be modified after the subnet has been created

name.description: Specify the subnet name.

from_name.description: Name of the subnet to be renamed

gateway.description: Specify the gateway for the default route of the subnet.

ipspace.description: Specify the ipspace for the subnet. The default value for this parameter is the default IPspace, named 'Default'.

ip_ranges.description: Specify the list of IP address ranges associated with the subnet.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_net_vlan.description: Create/Modify/Delete network VLAN Modify VLAN are supported only with REST broadcast_domain, ipspace and enabled keys are supported with REST and is ignored with ZAPI

state.description: Whether the specified network VLAN should exist or not

parent_interface.description: The interface that hosts the VLAN interface.

vlanid.description: The VLAN id. Ranges from 1 to 4094.

broadcast_domain.description: Specify the broadcast_domain name. Only supported with REST and is ignored with ZAPI. Required with 9.6 and 9.7, but optional with 9.8 or later.

ipspace.description: Specify the ipspace for the broadcast domain. Only supported with REST and is ignored with ZAPI. Required with 9.6 and 9.7, but optional with 9.8 or later.

enabled.description: Enable/Disable Net vlan. Only supported with REST and is ignored with ZAPI.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_nfs.description: Enable or disable NFS on ONTAP

state.description: Whether NFS should exist or not.

service_state.description: Whether the specified NFS should be enabled or disabled. Creates NFS service if doesnt exist.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

nfsv3_fsid_change.description: status of if NFSv3 clients see change in FSID as they traverse filesystems. REST support added in collection version 23.2.0 and requires ONTAP 9.11.0 or later.

nfsv4_fsid_change.description: status of if NFSv4 clients see change in FSID as they traverse filesystems. REST support added in collection version 23.2.0 and requires ONTAP 9.13.1 or later.

nfsv41.description: status of NFSv41. usage of C(nfsv4.1) is deprecated as it does not match Ansible naming convention.  The alias will be removed. please use C(nfsv41) exclusively for this option.

nfsv4_id_domain.description: Name of the nfsv4_id_domain to use.

nfsv40_acl.description: status of NFS v4.0 ACL feature

nfsv40_read_delegation.description: status for NFS v4.0 read delegation feature.

nfsv40_write_delegation.description: status for NFS v4.0 write delegation feature.

nfsv41_acl.description: status of NFS v4.1 ACL feature

nfsv41_read_delegation.description: status for NFS v4.1 read delegation feature.

nfsv41_write_delegation.description: status for NFS v4.1 write delegation feature.

nfsv40_referrals.description: status for NFS v4.0 referrals. REST support added in collection version 23.2.0 and requires ONTAP 9.13.1 or later.

nfsv41_referrals.description: status for NFS v4.1 referrals. REST support added in collection version 23.2.0 and requires ONTAP 9.13.1 or later.

showmount.description: Whether SVM allows showmount. With REST, supported from ONTAP 9.8 version.

tcp_max_xfer_size.description: TCP Maximum Transfer Size (bytes). The default value is 65536. This option requires ONTAP 9.11.0 or later in REST.

windows.description: This option can be set or modified when using REST. It requires ONTAP 9.11.0 or later.

root.description: This option can be set or modified when using REST. It requires ONTAP 9.11.0 or later.

security.description: This option can be set or modified when using REST. It requires ONTAP 9.11.0 or later.

nfsv3_hide_snapdir.description: Specifies whether hiding a snapshot directory under a NFSv3 mount point is enabled (support from ONTAP 9.13 onward). Supported only with REST.

nfsv3_mount_root_only.description: Specifies whether the SVM allows MOUNT protocol calls only from privileged ports (port numbers less than 1024). Supported only with REST.

nfsv3_ejukebox_enabled.description: Specifies whether NFSv3 EJUKEBOX error is enabled. Supported only with REST.

nfsv3_connection_drop.description: Specifies whether the dropping of a connection when an NFSv3 request is dropped is enabled. Supported only with REST.

nfsv3_64bit_identifiers_enabled.description: Specifies whether 64-bit support for NFSv3 FSIDs and file IDs is enabled. Supported only with REST.

nfsv4_64bit_identifiers_enabled.description: Specifies whether 64-bit support for NFSv4.x FSIDs and file IDs is enabled. Supported only with REST.

nfsv42_xattrs_enabled.description: Specifies whether NFSv4.2 or later extended attributes is enabled. Supported only with REST.

nfsv42_seclabel_enabled.description: Specifies whether NFSv4.2 or later security label is enabled. Supported only with REST.

nfsv40_acl_preserve.description: Specifies if the NFSv4 ACL is preserved or dropped when chmod is performed. In unified security style, this parameter also specifies if NTFS file permissions are preserved or dropped when chmod, chgrp, or chown are performed. Supported only with REST.

nfsv4_grace_seconds.description: Specifies the grace period for clients to reclaim file locks after a server failure. Supported only with REST.

nfsv4_lease_seconds.description: Specifies the lease seconds of the NFSv4 clients. If it is inactive for more than the time displayed, all of the file lock states on a node might be lost Supported only with REST.

credential_cache.description: This option can be set or modified when using REST. If not specified in POST, ONTAP uses default values for the TTLs. It requires ONTAP 9.11.0 or later.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_node.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Modify or Rename a node.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_node.description: Modify or Rename an ONTAP node.

name.description: The name for the node

from_name.description: The name of the node to be renamed.  If I(name) already exists, no action will be performed.

asset_tag.description: The asset tag for the node, not supported by REST

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ntfs_sd.short_description: NetApp ONTAP create, delete or modify NTFS security descriptor
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ntfs_sd.description: Create, modify or destroy NTFS security descriptor

state.description: Whether the specified NTFS security descriptor should exist or not.

vserver.description: Specifies the vserver for the NTFS security descriptor.

name.description: Specifies the NTFS security descriptor name. Not modifiable.

owner.description: Specifies the owner's SID or domain account of the NTFS security descriptor. Need to provide the full path of the owner.

group.description: Specifies the group's SID or domain account of the NTFS security descriptor. Need to provide the full path of the group.

control_flags_raw.description: Specifies the security descriptor control flags. 1... .... .... .... = Self Relative .0.. .... .... .... = RM Control Valid ..0. .... .... .... = SACL Protected ...0 .... .... .... = DACL Protected .... 0... .... .... = SACL Inherited .... .0.. .... .... = DACL Inherited .... ..0. .... .... = SACL Inherit Required .... ...0 .... .... = DACL Inherit Required .... .... ..0. .... = SACL Defaulted .... .... ...0 .... = SACL Present .... .... .... 0... = DACL Defaulted .... .... .... .1.. = DACL Present .... .... .... ..0. = Group Defaulted .... .... .... ...0 = Owner Defaulted At present only the following flags are honored. Others are ignored. ..0. .... .... .... = SACL Protected ...0 .... .... .... = DACL Protected .... .... ..0. .... = SACL Defaulted .... .... .... 0... = DACL Defaulted .... .... .... ..0. = Group Defaulted .... .... .... ...0 = Owner Defaulted Convert the 16 bit binary flags and convert to decimal for the input.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ntp.description: Create or delete or modify NTP server in ONTAP

state.description: Whether the specified NTP server should exist or not.

server_name.description: The name of the NTP server to manage.

key_id.description: The symmetric authentication key ID being used for this time server.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ntp_key.description: Create or delete or modify NTP key in ONTAP

state.description: Whether the specified NTP key should exist or not.

id.description: NTP symmetric authentication key ID. The ID must be in the range 1 to 65535.

digest_type.description: NTP symmetric authentication key type. Only SHA1 currently supported.

value.description: NTP symmetric authentication key value. The value must be exactly 40 hexadecimal digits for SHA1 keys.

state.description: Whether the specified NVMe should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

status_admin.description: Whether the status of NVMe should be up or down

state.description: Whether the specified namespace should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

size_unit.description: The unit used to interpret the size parameter.

path.description: Namespace path. The name of the NVMe namespace. NVMe namespace names are paths of the form "/vol/<volume>[/<qtree>]/<namespace>" where the qtree name is optional. For ASA R2 systems, The path should match the format <name>[@<snapshot-name>].

block_size.description: Size in bytes of a logical block. Possible values are 512 (Data ONTAP 9.6 and later), 4096. The default value is 4096.

provisioning_options.description: Options that are applied to the operation. This option is available only for ASA R2 systems.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_nvme_subsystem.description: Create/Delete NVME subsystem Associate(modify) host/map to NVME subsystem NVMe service should be existing in the data vserver with NVMe protocol as a pre-requisite

state.description: Whether the specified subsystem should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

skip_host_check.description: Skip host check Required to delete an NVMe Subsystem with attached NVMe namespaces

skip_mapped_check.description: Skip mapped namespace check Required to delete an NVMe Subsystem with attached NVMe namespaces

hosts.description: List of host NQNs (NVMe Qualification Name) associated to the controller.

paths.description: List of Namespace paths to be associated with the subsystem. For ASA R2 systems, The paths should match the format <name>[@<snapshot-name>].

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_object_store.short_description: NetApp ONTAP manage object store config.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_object_store.description: Create or delete object store config on ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified object store config should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the object store config to manage.

provider_type.description: The name of the object store config provider.

server.description: Fully qualified domain name of the object store config.

port.description: Port number of the object store that ONTAP uses when establishing a connection.

container.description: Data bucket/container name used in S3 requests.

access_key.description: Access key ID for AWS_S3 and SGWS provider types.

secret_password.description: Secret access key for AWS_S3 and SGWS provider types.

certificate_validation_enabled.description: Is SSL/TLS certificate validation enabled? If not specified, ONTAP will default to true.

ssl_enabled.description: Is SSL enabled? If not specified, ONTAP will default to true.

change_password.description: By default, the secret_password is used on create but ignored if the resource already exists. If set to true, the module always attempt to change the paswword as it cannot read the current value. When this is set to true, the module is not idempotent.

owner.description: Owner of the target.  Cannot be modifed. With REST, allowed values are fabricpool or snapmirror.  A target can be used by only one feature. With ZAPI, the only allowed value is fabricpool. If absent, fabricpool is assumed on creation.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_partitions.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Assign partitions and disks to nodes.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_partitions.description: Assign the specified number of partitions or disks eligible for partitioning to a node. There is some overlap between this module and the na_ontap_disks module. If you don't have ADP v1 or v2 then you should be using the na_ontap_disks module to assign whole disks. Partitions/disks are added in the following order 1.    Any unassigned partitions are added. 2.    Any unassigned disks of the correct type are added and will be partitioned when added to an aggregate if required. 3.    Any spare partner partitions will be re-assigned. 4.    Any partner spare disks will be re-assigned and be partitioned when added to an aggregate. If you specify a partition_count less than the current number of partitions, then spare partitions will be unassigned. If a previously partitioned disk has the partitions removed, and even if it is "slow zeroed" the system \ will consider it a shared partitioned disk rather than a spare. In a root-data-data configuration (ADPv2) if you specify data1 as the partition_type then only P1 partitions will be counted. Disk autoassign must be turned off before using this module to prevent the disks being reassigned automatically by the cluster. This can be done through na_ontap_disk_options or via the cli "disk option modify -node <node_name> -autoassign off".

node.description: Specifies the node that the partitions and disks should be assigned to.

partition_count.description: Total number of partitions that should be assigned to the owner.

disk_type.description: The type of disk that the partitions that should use.

partition_type.description: The type of partiton being assigned either root, data, data1 or data2,

partitioning_method.description: The type of partiton method being used, either root_data or root_data1_data2.

min_spares.description: Minimum spares disks or partitions required per type for the node.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_ports.description: Add or remove ports for broadcast domain and portset.

state.description: Whether the specified port should be added or removed.

vserver.description: Name of the SVM. Specify this option when operating on portset.

resource_name.description: name of the portset or broadcast domain.

resource_type.description: type of the resource to add a port to or remove a port from. adding or removing ports in portset requires ONTAP version 9.9 or later in REST

ipspace.description: Specify the required ipspace for the broadcast domain. A domain ipspace can not be modified after the domain has been created.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_portset.description: Create/Delete ONTAP portset, modify ports in a portset. Modify type(protocol) is not supported in ONTAP.

state.description: If you want to create a portset.

vserver.description: Name of the SVM.

name.description: Name of the port set to create.

type.description: Required for create in ZAPI. Default value is mixed if not specified at the time of creation in REST. Protocols accepted for this portset.

force.description: If 'false' or not specified, the request will fail if there are any igroups bound to this portset. If 'true', forcibly destroy the portset, even if there are existing igroup bindings.

ports.description: Specify the ports associated with this portset. Should be comma separated. It represents the expected state of a list of ports at any time, and replaces the current value of ports. Adds a port if it is specified in expected state but not in current state. Deletes a port if it is in current state but not in expected state.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_publickey.description: Add, modify, or remove publickeys. Requires ONTAP 9.7 or later, and only supports REST.

state.description: Whether the specified publickey should exist or not.

account.description: The name of the user account.

comment.description: Optional comment for the public key.

delete_all.description: If index is not present, with state=absent, delete all public key for this user account.

index.description: Index number for the public key. If index is not present, with state=present, the public key is always added, using the next available index. If index is not present, with state=present, the module is not idempotent. If index is not present, with state=absent, if only one key is found, it is deleted.  Otherwise an error is reported. See also C(delete_all) option.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use. Omit this option for cluster scoped user accounts.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_qos_adaptive_polic.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Adaptive Quality of Service policy group.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_qos_adaptive_polic.description: Create, destroy, modify, or rename an Adaptive QoS policy group on NetApp ONTAP. Module is based on the standard QoS policy group module.

state.description: Whether the specified policy group should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the policy group to manage.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

from_name.description: Name of the existing policy group to be renamed to name.

absolute_min_iops.description: Absolute minimum IOPS defined by this policy.

expected_iops.description: Minimum expected IOPS defined by this policy.

peak_iops.description: Maximum possible IOPS per allocated or used TB|GB.

peak_iops_allocation.description: Whether peak_iops is specified by allocated or used space.

force.description: Setting to 'true' forces the deletion of the workloads associated with the policy group along with the policy group.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_qos_policy_group.short_description: NetApp ONTAP manage policy group in Quality of Service.
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_qos_policy_group.description: Create, destroy, modify, or rename QoS policy group on NetApp ONTAP. With ZAPI, only fixed QoS policy group is supported. With REST, both fixed and adaptive QoS policy group are supported.

state.description: Whether the specified policy group should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the policy group to manage.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

from_name.description: Name of the existing policy group to be renamed to name.

max_throughput.description: Maximum throughput defined by this policy. Not supported with REST, use C(fixed_qos_options).

min_throughput.description: Minimum throughput defined by this policy. Not supported with REST, use C(fixed_qos_options).

is_shared.description: Whether the SLOs of the policy group are shared between the workloads or if the SLOs are applied separately to each workload. Not supported with REST, use C(fixed_qos_options).

force.description: Setting to 'true' forces the deletion of the workloads associated with the policy group along with the policy group. Not supported with REST.

fixed_qos_options.description: Set Minimum and Maximum throughput defined by this policy. Only supported with REST. Required one of throughtput options when creating qos_policy.

adaptive_qos_options.description: Adaptive QoS policy-groups define measurable service level objectives (SLOs) that adjust based on the storage object used space and the storage object allocated space. Only supported with REST.

state.description: Whether the specified qtree should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the qtree to manage. With REST, this can also be a path.

from_name.description: Name of the qtree to be renamed.

flexvol_name.description: The name of the FlexVol the qtree should exist on.

vserver.description: The name of the vserver to use.

export_policy.description: The name of the export policy to apply.

security_style.description: The security style for the qtree.

oplocks.description: Whether the oplocks should be enabled or not for the qtree.

unix_permissions.description: File permissions bits of the qtree. Accepts either octal or string format. Examples 0777, 777 in octal and ---rwxrwxrwx, sstrwxrwxrwx, rwxrwxrwx in string format. Optional parameter. Only relevant for Unix and mixed security styles. Ignored when security_style is set to 'ntfs'.

force_delete.description: Whether the qtree should be deleted even if files still exist. Note that the default of true reflect the REST API behavior. a value of false is not supported with REST.

wait_for_completion.description: Only applicable for REST.  When using ZAPI, the deletion is always synchronous. Deleting a qtree may take time if many files need to be deleted. Set this parameter to 'true' for synchronous execution during delete. Set this parameter to 'false' for asynchronous execution. For asynchronous, execution exits as soon as the request is sent, and the qtree is deleted in background.

time_out.description: Maximum time to wait for qtree deletion in seconds when wait_for_completion is True. Error out if task is not completed in defined time. Default is set to 3 minutes.

unix_user.description: The user set as owner of the qtree. Optional parameter. Only relevant for Unix and mixed security styles. Ignored when security_style is set to 'ntfs'. Only supported with REST and ONTAP 9.9 or later.

unix_group.description: The group set as owner of the qtree. Optional parameter. Only relevant for Unix and mixed security styles. Ignored when security_style is set to 'ntfs'. Only supported with REST and ONTAP 9.9 or later.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_quota_policy.short_description: NetApp Ontap create, assign, rename or delete quota policy
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_quota_policy.description: Create, assign, rename or delete the quota policy.

state.description: Whether the specified quota policy should exist or not.

vserver.description: Specifies the vserver for the quota policy.

name.description: Specifies the quota policy name to create or rename to.

from_name.description: Name of the existing quota policy to be renamed to name.

auto_assign.description: when true, assign the policy to the vserver, whether it is newly created, renamed, or already exists. when true, the policy identified by name replaces the already assigned policy. when false, the policy is created if it does not already exist but is not assigned.

state.description: Whether the specified quota should exist or not.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

volume.description: The name of the volume that the quota resides on.

quota_target.description: The quota target of the type specified. users and group takes quota_target value in REST. For default user and group quota rules, the quota_target must be specified as "".

qtree.description: Name of the qtree for the quota. For user or group rules, it can be the qtree name or "" if no qtree. For tree type rules, this field must be "".

policy.description: Name of the quota policy from which the quota rule should be obtained. Only supported with ZAPI. Multiple alternative quota policies (active and backup) are not supported in REST. REST manages the quota rules of the active policy.

set_quota_status.description: Whether the specified volume should have quota status on or off.

perform_user_mapping.description: Whether quota management will perform user mapping for the user specified in quota-target. User mapping can be specified only for a user quota rule.

file_limit.description: The number of files that the target can have. use '-' to reset file limit.

disk_limit.description: The amount of disk space that is reserved for the target. Expects a number followed with B (for bytes), KB, MB, GB, TB. If the unit is not present KB is used by default. Examples - 10MB, 20GB, 1TB, 20B, 10. In REST, if limit is less than 1024 bytes, the value is rounded up to 1024 bytes. use '-' to reset disk limit.

soft_file_limit.description: The number of files the target would have to exceed before a message is logged and an SNMP trap is generated. use '-' to reset soft file limit.

soft_disk_limit.description: The amount of disk space the target would have to exceed before a message is logged and an SNMP trap is generated. See C(disk_limit) for format description. In REST, if limit is less than 1024 bytes, the value is rounded up to 1024 bytes. use '-' to reset soft disk limit.

threshold.description: The amount of disk space the target would have to exceed before a message is logged. See C(disk_limit) for format description. Only supported with ZAPI.

activate_quota_on_change.description: Method to use to activate quota on a change. Default value is 'resize' in ZAPI. With REST, Changes to quota rule limits C(file_limit), C(disk_limit), C(soft_file_limit), and C(soft_disk_limit) are applied automatically without requiring a quota resize operation.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_rest_cli.short_description: NetApp ONTAP run any CLI command using REST api/private/cli/

autosupport_messages_history support/auto-update support/auto-update/configurations support/auto-update/updates support/configuration-backup support/configuration-backup/backups support/coredump/coredumps support/ems or support_ems_config support/ems/destinations or event_notification_info or event_notification_destination_info support/ems/events or support_ems_events support/ems/filters or support_ems_filters support/ems/messages support/snmp support/snmp/traphosts support/snmp/users svm/migrations svm/peers or svm_peers_info or vserver_peer_info svm/peer-permissions or svm_peer-permissions_info svm/svms or vserver_info B(The following do not have direct Rest API equivalent) aggr_efficiency_info cifs_vserver_security_info clock_info cluster_identity_info net_vlan_info sis_info snapmirror_destination_info system_node_info volume_space_info Can specify a list of values to include a larger subset. REST APIs are supported with ONTAP 9.6 onwards.

max_records.description: Maximum number of records returned in a single call.

fields.description: Request specific fields from subset. Recommended - '<list of fields>' to return specified fields, only one subset will be allowed. Discouraged - '*' to return all the fields, one or more subsets are allowed. This option can be used for discovery, but is discouraged in production. Stongly discouraged - '**' to return all the fields, one or more subsets are allowed. This option can put an extra load on the system and should not be used in production. Limited - '' to return default fields, generally the properties that uniquely identify the record (keys). Other data is not returned by default and need to be explicitly called for using the field name or *. If the option is not present, return default fields for that API (see '' above).

parameters.description: Allows for any rest option to be passed in

use_python_keys.description: If true, I(/) in the returned dictionary keys are translated to I(_). It makes it possible to use a . notation when processing the output. For instance I(ontap_info["svm/svms"]) can be accessed as I(ontap_info.svm_svms).

owning_resource.description: Some resources cannot be accessed directly.  You need to select them based on the owner or parent.  For instance, volume for a snapshot. The following subsets require an owning resource, and the following suboptions when uuid is not present. <application/consistency-groups/metrics> B(cg_name) is the consistency group name, B(svm_name) is the owning vserver name for the consistency group. <application/consistency-groups/snapshots> B(cg_name) is the consistency group name, B(svm_name) is the owning vserver name for the consistency group. <storage/volumes/snapshots>  B(volume_name) is the volume name, B(svm_name) is the owning vserver name for the volume. <protocols/nfs/export-policies/rules> B(policy_name) is the name of the policy, B(svm_name) is the owning vserver name for the policy, B(rule_index) is the rule index. <protocols/vscan/on-access-policies> B(svm_name) is the owning vserver name for the vscan. <protocols/vscan/on-demand-policies> B(svm_name) is the owning vserver name for the vscan. <protocols/vscan/scanner-pools> B(svm_name) is the owning vserver name for the vscan.

ignore_api_errors.description: List of substrings. If a substring is contained in an error message when fetching a subset, the module does not fail and the error is reported in the subset.

hal_linking.description: if false, HAL-encoded links are disabled in the REST calls.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_restit.short_description: NetApp ONTAP Run any REST API on ONTAP
netapp.ontap.na_ontap_restit.description: Call a REST API on ONTAP. Cluster REST API are run using a cluster admin account. Vserver REST API can be run using a vsadmin account or using vserver tunneling (cluster admin with I(vserver_) options). In case of success, a json dictionary is returned as C(response). In case of a REST API error, C(status_code), C(error_code), C(error_message) are set to help with diagnosing the issue, and the call is reported as an error ('failed'). Other errors (eg connection issues) are reported as Ansible error.

api.description: The REST API to call (eg I(cluster/software), I(svms/svm)).

query.description: A list of dictionaries for the query parameters

body.description: A dictionary for the info parameter

vserver_name.description: if provided, forces vserver tunneling.  username identifies a cluster admin account.

vserver_uuid.description: if provided, forces vserver tunneling.  username identifies a cluster admin account.

hal_linking.description: if true, HAL-encoded links are returned in the response.

wait_for_completion.description: when true, POST/PATCH/DELETE can be handled synchronously and asynchronously. if the response indicates that a job is in progress, the job status is checked periodically until is completes. when false, the call returns immediately.

files.description: A dictionary for the parameters when using multipart/form-data. This is very infrequently needed, but required to write a file (see examples) When present, requests will automatically set the Content-Type header to multipart/form-data.

accept_header.description: Value for the Accept request HTTP header. This is very infrequently needed, but required to read a file (see examples). For most cases, omit this field.  Set it to "multipart/form-data" when expecting such a format. By default the module is using "application/json" or "application/hal+json" when hal_linking is true.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_s3_buckets.description: Create, delete, or modify S3 buckets on NetApp ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified S3 bucket should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the S3 or NAS bucket.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

aggregates.description: List of aggregates names to use for the S3 bucket. This option is not supported when I(type=nas).

constituents_per_aggregate.description: Number of constituents per aggregate. This option is not supported when I(type=nas).

size.description: Size of the S3 bucket in bytes. This option is not supported when I(type=nas).

type.description: Specifies the bucket type. Valid values are "s3"and "nas".

nas_path.description: Specifies the NAS path to which the nas bucket corresponds to.

versioning_state.description: Specifies the versioning state of the bucket. The versioning state cannot be modified to 'disabled' from any other state. Requires ONTAP 9.11.1 or later.

policy.description: Access policy uses the Amazon Web Services (AWS) policy language syntax to allow S3 tenants to create access policies to their data

qos_policy.description: A policy group defines measurable service level objectives (SLOs) that apply to the storage objects with which the policy group is associated. If you do not assign a policy group to a bucket, the system wil not monitor and control the traffic to it. This option is not supported when I(type=nas).

audit_event_selector.description: Audit event selector allows you to specify access and permission types to audit. This option is not supported when I(type=nas).

snapshot_policy.description: Specifies the snapshot policy for the bucket.

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_s3_groups.description: Create, delete, or modify S3 groups on NetApp ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified S3 group should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the S3 group.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

users.description: List of users to to add the the group

policies.description: Policies to add the the group

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_s3_policies.description: Create, delete, or modify S3 Policies on NetApp ONTAP.

state.description: Whether the specified S3 policy should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the S3 policy.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

statements.description: Policy statements are built using this structure to specify permissions Grant <Effect> to allow/deny <Principal> to perform <Action> on <Resource> when <Condition> applies

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_s3_services.description: Create, delete or modify S3 Service

state.description: Whether the specified S3 bucket should exist or not.

name.description: The name of the S3 service.

vserver.description: Name of the vserver to use.

is_http_enabled.description: Specifies whether HTTP is enabled on the S3 server being created or modified

is_https_enabled.description: Specifies whether HTTPS is enabled on the S3 server being created or modified

port.description: Specifies the HTTP listener port for the S3 server

secure_port.description: Specifies the HTTPS listener port for the S3 server

certificate_name.description: name of https certificate to use for the service

lambda_config.description: Configuration parameters for AWS Lambda proxy functionality. These option and suboptions are only supported with REST.

netapp.ontap.na_ontap_s3_users.description: Create, delete, or modify S3 users on NetApp ONTAP.
